policies.policy_title: Basel III: A global regulatory framework for more resilient banks and banking systems
policies.policy_description: This document presents the Basel Committee’s reforms to strengthen global capital and liquidity rules with the goal of promoting a more resilient banking sector.
policies.scope: Global banking sector
policies.objective: To improve the banking sector’s ability to absorb shocks arising from financial and economic stress.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The Basel Committee is raising the resilience of the banking sector by strengthening the regulatory capital framework, enhancing the quality and quantity of the regulatory capital base.
subpolicies.control: Includes a leverage ratio as a backstop to risk-based capital measures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The reforms strengthen bank-level regulation to improve resilience during stress and address system-wide risks.
subpolicies.control: Interrelated approaches to supervision to reduce the risk of system-wide shocks.

policies.policy_title: Raising the Quality, Consistency and Transparency of the Capital Base
policies.policy_description: This policy aims to ensure that banks' risk exposures are backed by a high-quality capital base, addressing systemic risks and enhancing market discipline.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banks and financial institutions.
policies.objective: To improve the quality and transparency of the capital base to mitigate systemic risks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The predominant form of Tier 1 capital must be common shares and retained earnings, with harmonized deductions and filters applied.
subpolicies.control: Ensure compliance with international standards for Tier 1 capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Phasing Out Innovative Hybrid Capital Instruments
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Innovative hybrid capital instruments with redemption incentives will be phased out, limited to 15% of the Tier 1 capital base.
subpolicies.control: Monitor and enforce the phase-out of qualifying hybrid instruments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Tier 2 capital instruments will be harmonized, and Tier 3 capital instruments, which only cover market risks, will be eliminated.
subpolicies.control: Ensure all Tier 2 instruments meet the new harmonized standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: All elements of capital must be disclosed along with a detailed reconciliation to the reported accounts to enhance market discipline.
subpolicies.control: Implement disclosure requirements for capital elements.

policies.policy_description: Framework for managing counterparty credit risk in banks using models.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks using models for counterparty credit risk management.
policies.objective: To ensure banks have sound and well-integrated risk management systems.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must conduct a regular program of backtesting comparing risk measures generated by the model against realized risk measures.
subpolicies.control: Regular backtesting must be documented and reviewed.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Initial validation and ongoing periodic review of IMM models must be independent of model developers.
subpolicies.control: Documentation of the validation process must be maintained.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The board of directors and senior management must be actively involved in the risk control process.
subpolicies.control: Daily reports must be reviewed by senior management.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The internal risk measurement model must be integrated into daily risk management processes.
subpolicies.control: Outputs must be part of planning, monitoring, and controlling risk profiles.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must ensure compliance with internal policies regarding the risk measurement system.
subpolicies.control: A risk management manual must document the principles and techniques used.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Regular independent reviews of the risk measurement system must be conducted.
subpolicies.control: Reviews should address adequacy of documentation and integration of risk measures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must document the process for initial and ongoing validation of their EPE models.
subpolicies.control: Documentation must allow third parties to recreate the analysis.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must define criteria to assess their EPE models and document processes for performance remediation.
subpolicies.control: Written policy must describe unacceptable performance determination.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must define how representative counterparty portfolios are constructed for validating EPE models.
subpolicies.control: Documentation of portfolio construction must be maintained.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Validation must assess more than a single statistic of the model distribution.
subpolicies.control: Multiple statistics must be evaluated during validation.

policies.policy_description: Framework for managing counterparty credit risk through validation and backtesting of EPE models.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banks using internal models for counterparty credit risk.
policies.objective: To ensure accurate measurement and management of counterparty credit risk exposures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must validate their EPE models and relevant risk measures out to time horizons commensurate with the maturity of trades.
subpolicies.control: Regular validation and assessment of model performance.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Static, historical backtesting on representative counterparty portfolios must be conducted at regular intervals.
subpolicies.control: Backtesting must identify poor performance in EPE model risk measures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: An independent unit must be responsible for the design and implementation of the counterparty credit risk management system.
subpolicies.control: Daily reports on risk measurement model outputs must be produced and analyzed.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: A more conservative measure than the regulatory EAD metric may be used with prior supervisory approval.
subpolicies.control: Regular validation of the conservatism of the measure.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Standardized inferred rating treatment for long-term exposures must be followed.
subpolicies.control: Risk weights based on specific assessments must be applied appropriately.

policies.scope: Applicable to banks under Basel III framework

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Recognition of credit protection provided to securitisation exposures and eligibility of guarantors under the Foundation IRB approach.
subpolicies.control: Requirements outlined in paragraphs 189 to 194 must be met.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must consistently use chosen ECAIs and their ratings for risk weighting and management, with conditions for unsolicited ratings.
subpolicies.control: National supervisory authorities may allow the use of unsolicited ratings if they meet quality standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Operation of the capital conservation buffer to ensure banks build up capital outside periods of stress.
subpolicies.control: Banks should hold capital buffers above regulatory minimum and reduce discretionary distributions to rebuild buffers.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Recommendations for banks to maintain capital buffers and strategies for rebuilding them after drawdowns.

policies.policy_description: The Basel Committee is raising the resilience of the banking sector by strengthening the regulatory capital framework, building on the three pillars of the Basel II framework.
policies.scope: Global banking sector
policies.objective: To enhance the quality and quantity of the regulatory capital base and improve risk coverage.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Raising the Quality, Consistency and Transparency of the Capital Base
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: It is critical that banks’ risk exposures are backed by a high quality capital base, ensuring consistency in the definition of capital across jurisdictions.
subpolicies.control: Common shares and retained earnings must be the predominant form of Tier 1 capital, with harmonized deductions and transparency requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Harmonization of Tier 1 and Tier 2 Capital Instruments
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The remainder of the Tier 1 capital base must comprise subordinated instruments with specific characteristics, while Tier 2 capital instruments will be harmonized.
subpolicies.control: Innovative hybrid capital instruments with an incentive to redeem will be phased out, and Tier 3 capital instruments will be eliminated.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: To improve market discipline, the transparency of the capital base will be enhanced.
subpolicies.control: All elements of capital must be disclosed along with a detailed reconciliation to the reported accounts.

section_info.title: Part 1: Minimum capital requirements and buffers

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: Establishes minimum capital requirements and buffers for banks to enhance resilience and risk coverage.
policies.scope: Applicable to internationally active banks
policies.objective: To strengthen the risk coverage of the capital framework and mitigate systemic risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Implements reforms to raise capital requirements for trading books and complex securitisation exposures.
subpolicies.control: Must be implemented by the end of 2011.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Strengthens capital requirements for counterparty credit exposures arising from derivatives and securities financing activities.
subpolicies.control: Banks must determine capital requirements using stressed inputs.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Imposes a capital charge for potential mark-to-market losses associated with counterparty credit deterioration.
subpolicies.control: Addresses CVA risk not covered under Basel II.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Strengthens standards for collateral management and initial margining for large and illiquid derivative exposures.
subpolicies.control: Longer margining periods required for regulatory capital determination.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Supports the establishment of strong standards for financial market infrastructures, including central counterparties.
subpolicies.control: Capitalisation of bank exposures to CCPs based on compliance with standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Raises standards for managing counterparty credit risk, including wrong-way risk.
subpolicies.control: Final guidance issued for sound backtesting of counterparty credit exposures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Introduces measures to reduce reliance on external ratings within the capital framework.
subpolicies.control: Banks must perform internal assessments of rated securitisation exposures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Introduces a leverage ratio to constrain excessive leverage in the banking sector.
subpolicies.control: Calculated comparably across jurisdictions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Aims to reduce procyclicality and promote countercyclical buffers to stabilize the financial system.
subpolicies.control: Addresses the amplification of financial shocks.

policies.policy_description: Measures to ensure banks are resilient to procyclical dynamics and serve as shock absorbers in the financial system.
policies.scope: Banking sector
policies.objective: To dampen excess cyclicality of minimum capital requirements and promote capital conservation.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Safeguards to address excess cyclicality of minimum capital requirements, including long-term data horizons and stress tests.
subpolicies.control: Use of long-term data, downturn LGD estimates, and stress testing.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Promoting stronger provisioning practices through an expected loss approach and updating supervisory guidance.
subpolicies.control: Support for IASB's expected loss approach and consistent supervisory guidance.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Framework to promote the conservation of capital and build adequate buffers above minimum requirements.
subpolicies.control: Implementation of capital conservation standards and supervisory tools.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Adjusting capital buffer range in response to excessive credit growth to protect the banking sector.
subpolicies.control: Countercyclical buffer mechanism to moderate excess credit growth.

policies.policy_description: Establishes minimum capital measures to address unexpected losses and build additional buffers above the minimum requirement.
policies.scope: Banking sector
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain adequate capital buffers to protect against severe shocks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires systemically important banks to have loss absorbing capacity beyond minimum standards to mitigate systemic risks.
subpolicies.control: Ongoing development of capital surcharges, contingent capital, and bail-in debt.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Introduces internationally harmonized liquidity standards to ensure banks maintain a strong liquidity base.
subpolicies.control: Establishes minimum liquidity requirements and promotes international consistency.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Promotes resilience to liquidity disruptions over a thirty-day horizon by ensuring banks have sufficient high-quality liquid assets.
subpolicies.control: Banks must maintain unencumbered, liquid assets to offset net cash outflows during acute stress scenarios.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires banks to maintain a minimum amount of stable funding relative to the liquidity profiles of their assets.
subpolicies.control: Encourages banks to fund activities with stable sources of funding on an ongoing basis.

policies.policy_description: Establishes minimum capital requirements and buffers for banking organizations to ensure resilience in the financial system.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banking organizations under the Basel III framework.
policies.objective: To enhance the stability and resilience of the banking sector by ensuring adequate capital levels.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires banks to maintain a minimum level of liquid assets to cover short-term liabilities.
subpolicies.control: LCR must be at least 100%.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Aims to promote stable funding structures by requiring banks to maintain a minimum amount of stable funding.
subpolicies.control: NSFR must be at least 100% by January 1, 2018.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks should conduct assessments to understand their liquidity needs based on contractual commitments.
subpolicies.control: Regular assessments to identify potential liquidity needs.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Involves analyzing concentrations of wholesale funding to assess liquidity risks.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring of funding sources and their withdrawal risks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Measures the amount of unencumbered assets available for secured funding.
subpolicies.control: Awareness of potential capacity to raise secured funds.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires assessment of LCR in significant currencies to manage currency exposure.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring of currency exposure levels.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Utilizes market data to monitor potential liquidity difficulties.
subpolicies.control: Access to instantaneous market data on asset prices and liquidity.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Introduces transitional measures to implement new capital standards while supporting lending.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring processes during the transition period.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines components of capital and sets minimum capital requirements.
subpolicies.control: Common Equity Tier 1 must be at least 4.5% of risk-weighted assets.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Details the elements that constitute total regulatory capital.
subpolicies.control: Tier 1 Capital must be at least 6.0% and Total Capital at least 8.0% of risk-weighted assets.

policies.policy_description: Establishes the criteria for the calculation of Common Equity Tier 1 and Additional Tier 1 capital for banks.
policies.scope: Applicable to internationally active banks structured as joint stock companies and other types of banks.
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain adequate capital levels to absorb losses and support financial stability.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Criteria for Common Equity Tier 1 Capital
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the criteria that instruments must meet to be classified as Common Equity Tier 1 capital.
subpolicies.control: Instruments must represent the most subordinated claim, be perpetual, and not create expectations of redemption.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Outlines the minimum criteria for instruments issued by banks to qualify as Additional Tier 1 capital.
subpolicies.control: Instruments must be subordinated, perpetual, and callable only under specific conditions with supervisory approval.

policies.policy_description: Establishes the minimum capital requirements and buffers for banks to ensure financial stability.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banking organizations under the Basel III framework.
policies.objective: To ensure that banks maintain adequate capital to absorb losses and support ongoing operations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Dividends/coupons must be paid out of distributable items and cannot have credit sensitive features.
subpolicies.control: Prohibits 'dividend pushers' and ensures full discretion to cancel distributions/payments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments classified as liabilities must have principal loss absorption mechanisms.
subpolicies.control: Requires conversion to common shares or a write-down mechanism at a pre-specified trigger point.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Neither the bank nor related parties can purchase the instrument or fund its purchase.
subpolicies.control: Ensures independence in capital raising.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments cannot have features that hinder recapitalisation.
subpolicies.control: Prohibits compensation provisions for lower-priced new instruments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the criteria for instruments to be included in Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Includes requirements for maturity, subordination, and call options.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: General provisions or loan-loss reserves qualify for inclusion within Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Provisions must be freely available to meet future losses.

policies.policy_description: Establishes the minimum capital requirements and buffers for banks, including the treatment of various capital instruments.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banks under the Basel III framework.
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain adequate capital to absorb losses and support financial stability.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Limits the inclusion of general provisions and loan-loss reserves in Tier 2 capital to a maximum of 1.25 percentage points of credit risk-weighted risk assets.
subpolicies.control: Compliance with the limits set under the Internal Ratings-based Approach.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Recognition of Minority Interest in Common Equity Tier 1
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Details the conditions under which minority interests from consolidated subsidiaries can be recognized in Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Instruments must meet criteria for classification as common shares and the subsidiary must be a bank.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Recognition of Tier 1 Capital from Subsidiaries
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Specifies the conditions for recognizing Tier 1 capital instruments issued by consolidated subsidiaries.
subpolicies.control: Instruments must meet criteria for classification as Tier 1 capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Recognition of Total Capital from Subsidiaries
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Establishes the criteria for recognizing total capital instruments issued by consolidated subsidiaries.
subpolicies.control: Instruments must meet criteria for classification as Tier 1 or Tier 2 capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Treatment of Capital Issued through Special Purpose Vehicles (SPVs)
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Outlines the conditions under which capital issued through SPVs can be included in Additional Tier 1 or Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Capital must meet relevant entry criteria and the SPV's only asset must be its investment in the bank's capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Sets forth the regulatory adjustments to be applied to regulatory capital, primarily affecting Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Adjustments include deductions for goodwill and other intangibles.

policies.policy_description: Regulations governing the minimum capital requirements and buffers for banks.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks reporting under local GAAP and IFRS.
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain adequate capital to support their operations and absorb losses.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: DTAs that rely on future profitability must be deducted in the calculation of Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Netting of DTAs with associated DTLs is permitted under specific conditions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The amount related to hedging items not fair valued must be derecognised in Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Positive amounts deducted and negative amounts added back.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Shortfall of Provisions to Expected Losses
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Full deduction from capital for shortfall of provisions under the IRB approach.
subpolicies.control: No reduction for tax effects expected from provisions rising to expected losses.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Gain on Sale Related to Securitisation Transactions
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Any increase in equity capital from securitisation transactions must be derecognised in Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Includes expected future margin income resulting in a gain-on-sale.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Defined Benefit Pension Fund Assets and Liabilities
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Pension fund liabilities must be fully recognised; assets deducted net of associated DTLs.
subpolicies.control: Offsetting assets may reduce deduction if they can be withdrawn from the fund.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: All investments in own common shares must be deducted in Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Includes contractual obligations to purchase own shares and specific netting rules.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Reciprocal cross holdings designed to inflate capital positions must be deducted in full.
subpolicies.control: Corresponding deduction approach applied to investments in the capital of other entities.

policies.policy_description: Regulatory adjustments for investments in the capital of banking, financial, and insurance entities outside the scope of regulatory consolidation.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and financial institutions regarding their capital investments.
policies.objective: To ensure that banks maintain adequate capital buffers and manage their investments in a manner that aligns with regulatory requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Investments in entities where the bank does not own more than 10% of issued common share capital are subject to specific regulatory adjustments.
subpolicies.control: Include direct, indirect, and synthetic holdings of capital instruments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: If total holdings exceed 10% of the bank's common equity, the excess must be deducted from the corresponding capital tier.
subpolicies.control: Apply a corresponding deduction approach based on the tier of capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Amounts below the threshold that are not deducted will continue to be risk weighted according to applicable rules.
subpolicies.control: Instruments in the trading book treated per market risk rules; banking book treated as per internal ratings-based or standardized approach.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Regulatory adjustments for investments where the bank owns more than 10% of issued common share capital or where the entity is an affiliate.
subpolicies.control: Holdings in both banking and trading books must be included.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Underwriting positions held for five working days or less can be excluded; longer positions must be included.
subpolicies.control: Monitor the duration of underwriting positions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Allows banks to exclude certain investments temporarily with prior supervisory approval.
subpolicies.control: Applicable in the context of resolving or providing financial assistance to distressed institutions.

policies.policy_description: Establishes the minimum capital requirements and buffers for banks to ensure financial stability.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banks under the Basel III framework.
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain adequate capital to absorb losses and support lending.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Items that may receive limited recognition when calculating Common Equity Tier 1, capped at 10% of common equity.
subpolicies.control: Includes significant investments in common shares, mortgage servicing rights, and deferred tax assets.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must disclose regulatory capital elements and adjustments to improve transparency.
subpolicies.control: Includes full reconciliation of capital elements, separate disclosure of adjustments, and descriptions of capital instruments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Phased implementation of new capital standards to support banking sector stability.
subpolicies.control: Includes gradual increase of Common Equity Tier 1 and Tier 1 capital requirements from 2013 to 2015.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Deductions and filters applied to Common Equity Tier 1 will be fully implemented by 2018.
subpolicies.control: Includes phased deductions for significant investments and other items affecting capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Gradual phase-out of capital instruments that no longer qualify as Tier 1 or Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Recognition of such instruments will be capped and reduced annually starting from 2013.

policies.policy_description: Defines the criteria and treatment of capital instruments for inclusion in Tier 1 and Tier 2 capital.
policies.scope: Applicable to financial institutions under Basel III framework.
policies.objective: To ensure that capital instruments meet the necessary criteria for regulatory recognition and to outline the treatment of instruments with redemption incentives.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Treatment of Instruments with Redemption Incentives Prior to 1 January 2013
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments with a call and a step-up prior to 1 January 2013 will continue to be recognized in Tier 1 or Tier 2 if they meet new criteria at maturity.
subpolicies.control: Instruments must be assessed on a forward-looking basis for compliance with new criteria.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Treatment of Instruments with Redemption Incentives On or After 1 January 2013
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments with a call and a step-up on or after 1 January 2013 will be phased out if they do not meet new criteria at maturity.
subpolicies.control: Instruments must be evaluated for qualification as Additional Tier 1 or Tier 2 prior to effective maturity.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Derecognition of Instruments Not Meeting New Criteria
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments that do not meet the new criteria for inclusion in Tier 1 or Tier 2 will be fully derecognized from 1 January 2013.
subpolicies.control: Instruments must be monitored for compliance with the new criteria continuously.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Capital instruments that do not meet the criteria for inclusion in Common Equity Tier 1 will be excluded from Common Equity Tier 1 as of 1 January 2013.
subpolicies.control: Instruments must be assessed against the specified conditions to determine eligibility.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Outlines the reforms to the counterparty credit risk framework effective on 1 January 2013 to capture all material risks.
subpolicies.control: Banks must implement revised metrics for counterparty credit risk using Effective EPE with stressed parameters.

policies.policy_description: Requirements for calculating the capital charge to cover the risk of mark-to-market losses on expected counterparty risk for OTC derivatives.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks calculating counterparty credit risk capital charges.
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain adequate capital against potential losses from counterparty credit risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Advanced CVA Risk Capital Charge for Banks with IMM Approval
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Calculation of the CVA capital charge by modeling the impact of changes in counterparties' credit spreads on CVAs of all OTC derivative counterparties.
subpolicies.control: Banks must use their approved VaR model for bonds and follow specified formulas for CVA calculation.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: CVA Risk Capital Charge Calculation for Non-IMM Netting Sets
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Inclusion of non-IMM netting sets into the CVA risk capital charge by assuming a constant EE profile.
subpolicies.control: Banks must set EE equal to the EAD as computed under CEM or SM for specified maturities.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Use of Proxy Spreads for CVA Calculation
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: When CDS spreads are unavailable, banks must use appropriate proxy spreads based on rating, industry, and region.
subpolicies.control: Banks must ensure the proxy spread reflects the market assessment of the counterparty.

policies.policy_description: Establishes the requirements for calculating capital charges related to counterparty credit risk and CVA.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks calculating CVA risk capital charges.
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain adequate capital buffers against potential CVA losses.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Details the method for calculating the CVA risk charge using standardized and advanced methods.
subpolicies.control: Banks must use supervisory approved methods for calculating CVA risk charges.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the types of hedges that can be included in the CVA risk capital charge calculation.
subpolicies.control: Only specific hedging instruments such as single-name CDSs and index CDSs are permitted.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Outlines the formula for banks without supervisory approval to calculate CVA capital charges.
subpolicies.control: Banks must follow the specified formula and weight mappings based on external ratings.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Aggregation of CCR and CVA Risk Charges
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Describes the process for aggregating default risk capital charges with CVA risk capital charges.
subpolicies.control: Outstanding EAD must be net of incurred CVA losses as specified.

policies.policy_description: Establishes the capital charges for counterparty credit risk (CCR) based on different approaches and conditions for banks.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks with IMM approval and market-risk internal-models approval.
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain adequate capital buffers against counterparty credit risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: CCR Capital Charge for Banks with IMM Approval and Market-Risk Internal-Models Approval
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Determines the total CCR capital charge as the sum of the higher IMM capital charge based on current or stressed parameter calibrations and the advanced CVA risk capital charge.
subpolicies.control: Requires demonstration of specific VaR model effects to national supervisor.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: CCR Capital Charge for Banks with IMM Approval and Without Specific Risk VaR Approval
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Determines the total CCR capital charge as the sum of the higher IMM capital charge based on current or stressed parameter calibrations and the standardised CVA risk capital charge.
subpolicies.control: No specific risk VaR approval required.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: CCR Capital Charge for All Other Banks
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Determines the total CCR capital charge as the sum of the CEM or SM based capital charge and the standardised CVA risk capital charge.
subpolicies.control: Dependent on the bank’s CCR approach.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must identify exposures that give rise to a greater degree of general wrong-way risk and conduct stress testing and scenario analyses.
subpolicies.control: Regular reporting to senior management and the Board on wrong way risks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires banks to have policies for identifying and managing specific wrong-way risk for each legal entity.
subpolicies.control: Different treatment for calculating EAD for exposures with identified specific wrong way risk.

policies.policy_description: Establishes minimum capital requirements and buffers for banks under various approaches.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks using Advanced, Foundation IRB, and Standardised Approach.
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain sufficient capital to cover risks associated with their exposures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Loss Given Default (LGD) for Swap Transactions
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Sets LGD to 100% for Advanced or Foundation IRB banks for specific swap transactions.
subpolicies.control: Applicable to transactions with identified wrong way risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Asset Value Correlation Multiplier for Large Financial Institutions
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Implements a multiplier of 1.25 for correlation parameters of exposures to financial institutions with total assets >= $100 billion.
subpolicies.control: Requires the use of the most recent audited financial statements to determine asset size.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Margin Period of Risk for Collateralised Counterparties
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Establishes supervisory floors for margin periods of risk based on transaction types and conditions.
subpolicies.control: Imposes specific supervisory floors for netting sets based on trade volume and collateral liquidity.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires banks to adjust margin periods of risk if there are multiple margin call disputes.
subpolicies.control: Margin period must be at least double the supervisory floor for netting sets with a history of disputes.

policies.policy_description: Establishes minimum holding periods for various capital market transactions and outlines the recognition of internal market risk models.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks engaged in capital market transactions.
policies.objective: To ensure adequate capital buffers and risk management practices in line with Basel II and Basel III standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the minimum holding periods for different transaction types including repo-style transactions, other capital market transactions, and secured lending.
subpolicies.control: Daily remargining and daily mark-to-market requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Outlines the criteria for recognition of internal market risk models for repo-style and similar transactions.
subpolicies.control: Minimum holding period adjustments based on liquidity and risk factors.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Describes the shortcut method for estimating Effective Expected Positive Exposure (EPE) for margined counterparties.
subpolicies.control: Incorporates collateral management and exposure calculations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Prohibits the capture of EAD reductions due to downgrade triggers in collateral agreements.
subpolicies.control: Ensures compliance with internal models method.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Improves operational performance of the collateral management unit within banks.
subpolicies.control: Daily margin call management, dispute resolution, and reporting requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Mandates regular independent reviews of the Counterparty Credit Risk (CCR) management system.
subpolicies.control: Includes auditing of collateral management processes and documentation adequacy.

policies.policy_description: Establishes minimum capital requirements and buffers for banks to manage counterparty credit risks.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks employing internal models for risk management.
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain sufficient capital and manage risks effectively.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Integration of counterparty credit risk measures into daily risk management.
subpolicies.control: Daily risk management practices.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Approval Process for Risk Pricing Models
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Approval process for risk pricing models and valuation systems used by front and back-office personnel.
subpolicies.control: Model approval and validation processes.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Validation of any significant change in the CCR measurement process.
subpolicies.control: Change management procedures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the scope of counterparty credit risks captured by the risk measurement model.
subpolicies.control: Risk measurement model specifications.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Ensures the integrity of the management information system.
subpolicies.control: Data integrity checks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Ensures the accuracy and completeness of counterparty credit risk data.
subpolicies.control: Data validation processes.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Legal Terms Reflection in Exposure Measurements
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Accurate reflection of legal terms in collateral and netting agreements into exposure measurements.
subpolicies.control: Legal compliance checks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Verification of the consistency, timeliness, and reliability of data sources used to run internal models.
subpolicies.control: Data source independence and validation.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Ensures the accuracy and appropriateness of volatility and correlation assumptions.
subpolicies.control: Model assumptions review.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Ensures the accuracy of valuation and risk transformation calculations.
subpolicies.control: Calculation accuracy checks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Verification of the model’s accuracy through frequent backtesting.
subpolicies.control: Backtesting procedures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Cash management policies must account for liquidity risks of potential incoming margin calls.
subpolicies.control: Liquidity risk management.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Supervisory Haircuts for Non-Cash OTC Collateral
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for banks to use supervisory haircuts when transforming non-cash OTC collateral into cash-equivalent.
subpolicies.control: Collateral valuation standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirement for banks to model non-cash collateral jointly with underlying securities for OTC derivatives.
subpolicies.control: Modeling requirements for collateral.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Ensures sufficient resources are devoted to the orderly operation of margin agreements.
subpolicies.control: Resource allocation for collateral management.

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: Establishes a global regulatory framework for banks and banking systems to enhance resilience.
policies.scope: Applicable to internationally active banks under Basel III.
policies.objective: To strengthen the risk coverage of the capital framework and mitigate systemic risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Implements reforms to raise capital requirements for trading books and complex securitisation exposures.
subpolicies.control: Requires banks to use stressed value-at-risk (VaR) for capital requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Strengthens capital requirements for counterparty credit exposures from derivatives and financing activities.
subpolicies.control: Banks must determine capital requirements using stressed inputs.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Introduces a leverage ratio to constrain excessive leverage in the banking sector.
subpolicies.control: Calculated comparably across jurisdictions to serve as a supplementary measure.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Addresses the procyclical amplification of financial shocks in the banking system.
subpolicies.control: Promotes countercyclical buffers to mitigate financial instability.

policies.policy_description: Measures introduced by the Basel Committee to make banks more resilient to procyclical dynamics.
policies.scope: Banking sector
policies.objective: Ensure the banking sector serves as a shock absorber and protects against periods of excess credit growth.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Safeguards to address excess cyclicality of the minimum capital requirement including long-term data for estimating probabilities of default and stress testing.
subpolicies.control: Use long-term data horizons, downturn loss-given-default estimates, and conduct stress tests.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Promotion of stronger provisioning practices through an expected loss approach and updated supervisory guidance.
subpolicies.control: Advocate for changes in accounting standards and provide supervisory guidance.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Framework to promote conservation of capital and build adequate buffers above minimum requirements.
subpolicies.control: Implement internationally agreed capital conservation standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Regime to adjust capital buffer ranges when credit levels are excessive to protect the banking sector.
subpolicies.control: Establish countercyclical buffers based on credit growth indicators.

policies.policy_description: Establishes requirements for capital measures to address cyclicality and systemic risk in the banking sector.
policies.scope: Global banking institutions
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain strong capital buffers to protect against unexpected losses and systemic risks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Distinction between minimum capital requirements and additional buffers to address cyclicality and unexpected losses.
subpolicies.control: Supervisory expectations for banks to maintain capital above minimum requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for loss absorbing capacity beyond minimum standards for systemically important banks.
subpolicies.control: Development of capital surcharges, contingent capital, and bail-in debt.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Introduction of globally harmonized liquidity standards to promote stability in the banking sector.
subpolicies.control: Minimum requirements for liquidity management and supervisory standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Promotes resilience to liquidity disruptions over a 30-day horizon by ensuring sufficient high-quality liquid assets.
subpolicies.control: Requirements for unencumbered, liquid assets to offset net cash outflows during stress scenarios.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires a minimum amount of stable sources of funding relative to the liquidity profiles of assets.
subpolicies.control: Incentives for banks to maintain a sustainable maturity structure of assets and liabilities.

policies.policy_description: Establishes the components and requirements for capital within banking organizations.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banking organizations under Basel III framework.
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain sufficient high-quality capital to support their operations and absorb losses.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the elements that constitute total regulatory capital, including Tier 1 and Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Elements include Common Equity Tier 1, Additional Tier 1, and Tier 2 Capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Sets the minimum capital ratios that banks must maintain relative to their risk-weighted assets.
subpolicies.control: Common Equity Tier 1 must be at least 4.5%, Tier 1 Capital at least 6.0%, and Total Capital at least 8.0%.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Specifies the elements that make up Common Equity Tier 1 capital.
subpolicies.control: Includes common shares, stock surplus, retained earnings, and other comprehensive income.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Outlines the transitional measures for implementing new capital standards.
subpolicies.control: Introduces timelines for LCR and NSFR implementation and monitoring processes.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Describes the metrics used to assess liquidity risk in banking organizations.
subpolicies.control: Includes metrics such as contractual maturity mismatch, concentration of funding, and available unencumbered assets.

policies.policy_description: Criteria and requirements for instruments to be classified as Common Equity Tier 1 and Additional Tier 1 capital.
policies.scope: Banks and financial institutions
policies.objective: To ensure that capital instruments meet specific criteria for regulatory capital purposes.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Criteria for Common Equity Tier 1 Capital
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria that must be met for an instrument to be included in Common Equity Tier 1 capital.
subpolicies.control: Instruments must represent the most subordinated claim, be perpetual, and meet various distribution and loss absorption criteria.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minimum criteria for an instrument issued by the bank to be included in Additional Tier 1 capital.
subpolicies.control: Instruments must be issued and paid-in, subordinated, perpetual, and have specific conditions regarding callable features and distributions.

policies.policy_description: Regulations and criteria for defining capital in banking organizations.
policies.scope: Applicable to banking organizations under Basel III framework.
policies.objective: To ensure that banks maintain adequate capital to absorb losses and support financial stability.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Dividends/coupons must be paid out of distributable items and cannot have credit sensitive features.
subpolicies.control: Prohibits instruments with dividend pushers.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments cannot contribute to liabilities exceeding assets as per national insolvency law.
subpolicies.control: Ensures compliance with balance sheet tests.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Replacement issues can occur concurrently with but not after the instrument is called.
subpolicies.control: Regulatory approval required for calls.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minimum refers to the regulator’s prescribed minimum requirement, which may exceed Basel III Pillar 1 minimum.
subpolicies.control: Compliance with higher regulatory standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments classified as liabilities must have principal loss absorption through conversion or write-down mechanisms.
subpolicies.control: Specifies effects of write-downs on claims and payments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Neither the bank nor related parties can purchase the instrument or fund its purchase.
subpolicies.control: Prevents conflicts of interest.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments cannot have features that hinder recapitalization.
subpolicies.control: Prohibits compensation provisions for lower-priced new instruments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Proceeds from instruments not issued by an operating entity must be immediately available to meet capital criteria.
subpolicies.control: Ensures liquidity and compliance with capital requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria for inclusion in Tier 2 capital, including issuance, subordination, and maturity requirements.
subpolicies.control: Defines minimum standards for Tier 2 capital instruments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: General Provisions for Loan Loss Reserves
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Provisions held against future losses qualify for inclusion within Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Allows flexibility in managing unidentified losses.

policies.policy_description: Defines the criteria for capital eligibility under different approaches and limits for provisions.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks calculating Tier 2 capital under the Basel II framework.
policies.objective: To ensure that only eligible provisions and interests are recognized in Tier 2 capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Limits general provisions eligible for inclusion in Tier 2 capital to a maximum of 1.25 percentage points of credit risk-weighted risk assets.
subpolicies.control: Compliance with the standardized approach for calculating credit risk-weighted assets.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Allows recognition of the difference between total expected loss and total eligible provisions in Tier 2 capital up to a maximum of 0.6% of credit risk-weighted assets.
subpolicies.control: National discretion may apply for a limit lower than 0.6%.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minority interest from common shares issued by consolidated subsidiaries may be recognized in Common Equity Tier 1 if certain conditions are met.
subpolicies.control: The instrument must meet all requirements as if issued by the bank.

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: A framework for enhancing the resilience of banks and banking systems by strengthening capital requirements and risk coverage.
policies.scope: International banking institutions
policies.objective: To minimize disruption to capital instruments and enhance risk coverage in the capital framework.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Strengthening the risk coverage of the capital framework to capture major on- and off-balance sheet risks.
subpolicies.control: Implementation of higher capital requirements for trading book and complex securitisation exposures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Strengthening capital requirements for counterparty credit exposures arising from derivatives and securities financing activities.
subpolicies.control: Banks must determine capital requirements using stressed inputs and apply capital charges for potential mark-to-market losses.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Introducing a leverage ratio to constrain excessive leverage in the banking sector.
subpolicies.control: The leverage ratio is calculated comparably across jurisdictions to mitigate destabilising deleveraging processes.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Mitigating the procyclical amplification of financial shocks through countercyclical buffers.
subpolicies.control: Implementation of measures to address procyclicality in capital requirements.

policies.policy_description: Measures introduced by the Basel Committee to enhance the resilience of banks against procyclical dynamics.
policies.scope: Banking sector
policies.objective: To ensure the banking sector acts as a shock absorber rather than a transmitter of risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Measures to dampen excess cyclicality of the minimum capital requirement.
subpolicies.control: Use long-term data horizons, downturn loss-given-default estimates, and conduct stress tests.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Promotion of stronger provisioning practices through an expected loss approach.
subpolicies.control: Update supervisory guidance and address incentives in the regulatory capital framework.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Framework to promote conservation of capital and build adequate buffers.
subpolicies.control: Implementation of internationally agreed capital conservation standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Regime to adjust capital buffer range when credit growth is excessive.
subpolicies.control: Establish a countercyclical buffer to protect the banking sector.

policies.policy_description: Focus on unexpected losses and the distinction between minimum capital requirements and additional buffers.
policies.scope: Banking Sector
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain strong capital buffers to protect against severe shocks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Addressing systemic risk through enhanced loss absorbing capacity for systemically important banks.
subpolicies.control: Development of capital surcharges, contingent capital, and bail-in debt.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Introduction of internationally harmonized global liquidity standards to ensure banking sector stability.
subpolicies.control: Establishment of minimum liquidity requirements and promotion of an international level playing field.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Promote resilience to potential liquidity disruptions over a thirty-day horizon.
subpolicies.control: Ensure banks hold sufficient high-quality liquid assets to offset net cash outflows during acute stress scenarios.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Require a minimum amount of stable sources of funding relative to the liquidity profiles of assets.
subpolicies.control: Encourage banks to fund activities with stable sources of funding on an ongoing basis.

policies.policy_description: Aims to limit over-reliance on short-term wholesale funding and encourage better assessment of liquidity risk across all on- and off-balance sheet items.
policies.scope: Banking organizations and financial sector
policies.objective: To ensure stability in funding sources and improve liquidity risk assessment.

policies.policy_description: Introduced to ensure that banks have sufficient high-quality liquid assets to survive a significant stress scenario lasting for 30 days.
policies.scope: Banking organizations
policies.objective: To promote short-term resilience of the liquidity risk profile of banks.

policies.policy_description: A set of quantitative measures to monitor liquidity risk profiles of banking organizations.
policies.scope: Banking organizations and supervisors
policies.objective: To ensure consistent international monitoring of liquidity risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Assessment to understand a bank's liquidity needs by evaluating contractual commitments.
subpolicies.control: Frequent assessments to compare liquidity risk profiles.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Analysis of concentrations of wholesale funding to assess liquidity risks.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring of funding sources and their potential withdrawal impacts.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Measurement of unencumbered assets available for collateral in secured funding.
subpolicies.control: Awareness of capacity to raise additional secured funds.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Assessment of LCR in each significant currency to monitor foreign exchange risk.
subpolicies.control: Management of overall currency exposure.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Use of market-wide data and institution-specific information to monitor liquidity difficulties.
subpolicies.control: Real-time data monitoring for liquidity management.

policies.policy_description: Introduces arrangements to implement new standards while supporting lending to the economy.
policies.scope: Banking sector
policies.objective: To ensure banks can meet higher capital standards through reasonable earnings retention.

policies.policy_description: Establishes the components and criteria for regulatory capital.
policies.scope: Banking organizations
policies.objective: To provide a consistent definition of capital across jurisdictions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Components of Common Equity Tier 1 capital including common shares and retained earnings.
subpolicies.control: Criteria for classification and regulatory adjustments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Includes Common Equity Tier 1 and Additional Tier 1 capital.
subpolicies.control: Must meet minimum capital requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Represents gone-concern capital.
subpolicies.control: Must meet specific criteria for inclusion.

policies.policy_description: Criteria for instruments to be included in Common Equity Tier 1 capital.
policies.scope: Applicable to internationally active banks structured as joint stock companies.
policies.objective: To ensure that instruments classified as Common Equity Tier 1 meet specific criteria for loss absorption and capital quality.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Criteria for Classification as Common Shares
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria that must be met for an instrument to be classified as common shares for regulatory capital purposes.
subpolicies.control: Includes criteria such as subordinated claims, perpetual principal, and non-obligatory distributions.

policies.policy_description: Criteria for instruments to be included in Additional Tier 1 capital.
policies.scope: Applicable to instruments issued by the bank and its consolidated subsidiaries.
policies.objective: To define the elements that constitute Additional Tier 1 capital and ensure compliance with regulatory standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Criteria for Inclusion in Additional Tier 1 Capital
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minimum set of criteria for an instrument issued by the bank to be included in Additional Tier 1 capital.
subpolicies.control: Includes criteria such as being issued and paid-in, subordinated to creditors, and having no maturity date.

policies.policy_description: Regulations and requirements for capital instruments and their classification under Basel III.
policies.scope: Applicable to banking organizations and their capital instruments.
policies.objective: To ensure that capital instruments meet the necessary criteria for inclusion in Tier 1 and Tier 2 capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria for instruments to be included in Additional Tier 1 capital.
subpolicies.control: Instruments must not have credit sensitive dividend features, must allow full discretion to cancel distributions, and must not hinder recapitalization.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria for instruments to be included in Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Instruments must be subordinated, have a minimum original maturity, and must not have features that enhance seniority.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Provisions or loan-loss reserves that qualify for inclusion within Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Provisions must be freely available to meet losses that materialize.

policies.policy_description: Regulations regarding the inclusion of general provisions and loan-loss reserves in Tier 2 capital.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks calculating credit risk-weighted assets under the standardized and Internal Ratings-based approaches.
policies.objective: To ensure that only eligible provisions are recognized in Tier 2 capital and to limit the amount of such provisions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: General provisions eligible for inclusion in Tier 2 are limited to a maximum of 1.25 percentage points of credit risk-weighted risk assets.
subpolicies.control: Compliance with the limit on general provisions as per the standardized approach.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks may recognize the difference between total expected loss and total eligible provisions in Tier 2 capital, up to a maximum of 0.6% of credit risk-weighted assets under the IRB approach.
subpolicies.control: Application of national discretion to potentially apply a limit lower than 0.6%.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minority interest from common shares issued by consolidated subsidiaries may be recognized in Common Equity Tier 1 if it meets specific criteria.
subpolicies.control: Assessment of the instrument's compliance with the requirements for recognition in Common Equity Tier 1.

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: Framework for enhancing the resilience of banks and banking systems through improved capital requirements and risk management.
policies.scope: International banking institutions
policies.objective: To strengthen the capital framework and mitigate systemic risks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Strengthening the risk coverage of the capital framework to capture major on- and off-balance sheet risks.
subpolicies.control: Implementation of higher capital requirements for trading book and complex securitisation exposures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Introducing reforms to strengthen capital requirements for counterparty credit exposures arising from derivatives and securities financing activities.
subpolicies.control: Banks must determine capital requirements using stressed inputs and apply capital charges for potential mark-to-market losses.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Introducing a leverage ratio to constrain excessive leverage in the banking sector.
subpolicies.control: Leverage ratio calculated consistently across jurisdictions to supplement risk-based measures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Mitigating the procyclical amplification of financial shocks through countercyclical buffers.
subpolicies.control: Implementation of measures to address procyclicality in capital requirements and risk management practices.

policies.policy_description: Measures introduced by the Basel Committee to enhance the resilience of banks against procyclical dynamics.
policies.scope: Banking sector
policies.objective: To ensure the banking sector serves as a shock absorber rather than a transmitter of risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Measures to dampen excess cyclicality of the minimum capital requirement.
subpolicies.control: Use of long-term data horizons, downturn loss-given-default estimates, and stress testing.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Promotion of stronger provisioning practices through an expected loss approach.
subpolicies.control: Updating supervisory guidance and addressing incentives in the regulatory capital framework.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Framework to promote the conservation of capital and build adequate buffers.
subpolicies.control: Implementation of internationally agreed capital conservation standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Regime to adjust capital buffer range in response to excessive credit growth.
subpolicies.control: Countercyclical buffer to protect the banking sector during periods of excess credit.

policies.policy_description: Requirements for banks to maintain capital buffers above minimum standards to mitigate risks and ensure stability.
policies.scope: Banking sector
policies.objective: To ensure banks have sufficient capital to absorb losses and manage systemic risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Systemically important banks must have loss absorbing capacity beyond minimum standards.
subpolicies.control: Ongoing development of methodologies and proposals by the Basel Committee and Financial Stability Board.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Introduction of internationally harmonized global liquidity standards to ensure banks manage liquidity prudently.
subpolicies.control: Implementation of the Liquidity Coverage Ratio (LCR) and Net Stable Funding Ratio (NSFR).

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Promotes resilience to potential liquidity disruptions over a thirty-day horizon.
subpolicies.control: Requires banks to hold sufficient high-quality liquid assets to offset net cash outflows during acute stress.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires a minimum amount of stable sources of funding relative to liquidity profiles of assets.
subpolicies.control: Ensures sustainable maturity structure of assets and liabilities.

policies.policy_description: Aims to limit over-reliance on short-term wholesale funding and encourage better assessment of liquidity risk across all on- and off-balance sheet items.
policies.scope: Applicable to banking organizations globally.
policies.objective: To ensure stable funding over a one-year horizon.

policies.policy_description: Introduced to ensure banks have sufficient high-quality liquid assets to survive a significant stress scenario lasting for 30 days.
policies.scope: Applicable to banking organizations globally.
policies.objective: To promote short-term resilience of the liquidity risk profile.

policies.policy_description: A set of common metrics developed to monitor the liquidity risk profiles of banking organizations.
policies.scope: Applicable to supervisors and banking organizations.
policies.objective: To introduce consistency in the assessment of liquidity risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks should frequently conduct an assessment to understand their liquidity needs.
subpolicies.control: Provides a baseline of contractual commitments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Involves analyzing concentrations of wholesale funding from specific counterparties.
subpolicies.control: Assists supervisors in assessing funding liquidity risks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Measures the amount of unencumbered assets that can be used as collateral for secured funding.
subpolicies.control: Increases awareness of potential capacity to raise additional secured funds.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Assesses the LCR in each significant currency to manage currency exposure.
subpolicies.control: Monitors overall level and trend of currency exposure.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Includes market-wide data on asset prices, liquidity, and institution-specific information.
subpolicies.control: Provides instantaneous data on potential liquidity difficulties.

policies.policy_description: Introduces transitional arrangements to implement new standards for capital requirements.
policies.scope: Applicable to the banking sector during the transition period.
policies.objective: To ensure the banking sector can meet higher capital standards while supporting lending.

policies.policy_description: Establishes the components and requirements for regulatory capital.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and banking groups.
policies.objective: To ensure sufficient levels of high-quality capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the minimum requirements for Common Equity Tier 1 and Additional Tier 1 capital.
subpolicies.control: Common Equity Tier 1 must be at least 4.5% of risk-weighted assets.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the requirements for Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Total Capital must be at least 8.0% of risk-weighted assets.

policies.policy_description: Criteria for instruments to be included in Common Equity Tier 1 capital.
policies.scope: Applicable to internationally active banks structured as joint stock companies.
policies.objective: To ensure that Common Equity Tier 1 capital meets specific criteria for loss absorption and equity classification.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Criteria for Classification as Common Shares
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minimum criteria that must be met for an instrument to be classified as common shares for regulatory capital purposes.
subpolicies.control: Includes criteria related to claim subordination, perpetual principal, distribution conditions, and loss absorption.

policies.policy_description: Criteria and elements that constitute Additional Tier 1 capital.
policies.scope: Applicable to instruments issued by the bank that meet Additional Tier 1 criteria.
policies.objective: To define the criteria for instruments to be included in Additional Tier 1 capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Criteria for Inclusion in Additional Tier 1 Capital
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minimum criteria that must be met for an instrument to be included in Additional Tier 1 capital.
subpolicies.control: Includes criteria related to issuance, subordination, security, perpetual nature, call options, and dividend discretion.

policies.policy_description: Regulations governing the characteristics and requirements for instruments classified as capital for banking organizations.
policies.scope: Applicable to banking organizations and their capital instruments.
policies.objective: To ensure that capital instruments meet specific criteria for inclusion in Tier 1 and Tier 2 capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Dividends/coupons must be paid out of distributable items and cannot have credit sensitive features.
subpolicies.control: Prohibition of 'dividend pushers' and requirement for full discretion to cancel distributions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments classified as liabilities must have principal loss absorption through conversion or write-down mechanisms.
subpolicies.control: Reduction of claims in liquidation and coupon payments upon write-down.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Neither the bank nor related parties can purchase the instrument or fund its purchase.
subpolicies.control: Ensures independence of capital instruments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments must not have features that hinder recapitalization.
subpolicies.control: Provisions against compensatory clauses for lower-priced new instruments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria for inclusion in Tier 2 capital, including issuance, subordination, and maturity requirements.
subpolicies.control: Instruments must be subordinated and have a minimum maturity of five years.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Investors must not have rights to accelerate repayment except in bankruptcy or liquidation.
subpolicies.control: Maintains stability of capital instruments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Stock surplus from instruments included in Tier 2 capital must meet specific eligibility criteria.
subpolicies.control: Ensures that only qualifying stock surplus contributes to Tier 2 capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Provisions for unidentified losses are included in Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Supports capital adequacy in the face of future losses.

policies.policy_title: Inclusion of General Provisions in Tier 2 Capital
policies.policy_description: Limits the inclusion of general provisions/general loan-loss reserves in Tier 2 capital to a maximum of 1.25 percentage points of credit risk-weighted risk assets under the standardized approach.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks calculating capital under the standardized approach
policies.objective: To ensure that the inclusion of provisions in Tier 2 capital is limited and regulated.

policies.policy_title: Recognition of Excess Provisions under the IRB Approach
policies.policy_description: Allows banks to recognize the difference between total expected loss and total eligible provisions in Tier 2 capital up to a maximum of 0.6% of credit risk-weighted assets under the IRB approach.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks using the Internal Ratings-based Approach
policies.objective: To provide a framework for recognizing excess provisions in Tier 2 capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Allows for a national discretion to apply a limit lower than 0.6% for recognition of excess provisions.
subpolicies.control: Regulatory oversight at the national level

policies.policy_title: Recognition of Minority Interest in Common Equity Tier 1
policies.policy_description: Minority interest from common shares issued by fully consolidated subsidiaries may be recognized in Common Equity Tier 1 if certain conditions are met.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks with fully consolidated subsidiaries
policies.objective: To define the conditions under which minority interests can be recognized in capital calculations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Conditions for Recognition of Minority Interest
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The instrument giving rise to the minority interest must meet all criteria applicable to instruments issued by the bank.
subpolicies.control: Compliance with capital instrument criteria

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Sets the requirement for addressing excess credit growth at zero during normal times, increasing only during periods of excessive credit availability.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Loss Absorbing Capacity for Systemically Important Banks

policies.policy_description: Sets minimum capital ratios for banks to maintain financial stability.

policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain sufficient capital to absorb losses.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Common Equity Tier 1 must be at least 4.5% of risk-weighted assets.
subpolicies.control: Ensures banks have a strong capital base.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Tier 1 Capital must be at least 6.0% of risk-weighted assets.
subpolicies.control: Maintains a buffer for banks against financial stress.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Total Capital must be at least 8.0% of risk-weighted assets.
subpolicies.control: Ensures overall capital adequacy for banks.

policies.policy_description: Criteria for instruments to be included in Common Equity Tier 1 capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Criteria for Classification as Common Shares
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minimum criteria that must be met for an instrument to be classified as common shares for regulatory capital purposes.
subpolicies.control: Includes criteria such as subordinated claims, perpetual principal, and distribution conditions.

policies.policy_description: Criteria for instruments to be included in Additional Tier 1 capital.
policies.scope: Applicable to instruments issued by the bank and its consolidated subsidiaries.
policies.objective: To define the minimum criteria for inclusion of instruments in Additional Tier 1 capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Criteria for Inclusion in Additional Tier 1 Capital
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minimum criteria that must be met for an instrument to be classified as Additional Tier 1 capital.
subpolicies.control: Includes criteria such as being issued and paid-in, subordinated status, and perpetual nature.

policies.policy_description: Regulations governing the limits and minimum requirements for capital instruments and their characteristics.
policies.scope: Applicable to banking organizations and their capital instruments.
policies.objective: To ensure that capital instruments meet specific criteria for inclusion in regulatory capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Dividends/coupons must be paid out of distributable items.
subpolicies.control: Compliance with distributable items regulation.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments cannot have a credit sensitive dividend feature.
subpolicies.control: Assessment of dividend features.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments must not contribute to liabilities exceeding assets as per national insolvency law.
subpolicies.control: Balance sheet compliance check.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Replacement issues can occur concurrently with, but not after, the instrument is called.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring of replacement issuance.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minimum refers to the regulator’s prescribed minimum requirement, which may exceed Basel III requirements.
subpolicies.control: Adherence to regulatory minimums.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments with dividend pushers are prohibited to ensure full discretion in canceling distributions.
subpolicies.control: Review of instrument features.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments classified as liabilities must have principal loss absorption through conversion or write-down mechanisms.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Neither the bank nor related parties can purchase the instrument or fund its purchase.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments must not have features that hinder recapitalization.
subpolicies.control: Assessment of instrument terms.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Proceeds from instruments not issued by an operating entity must be immediately available to an operating entity.
subpolicies.control: Verification of proceeds availability.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Stock surplus not eligible for Tier 1 can only be included in Tier 2 if shares are permitted for Tier 2.
subpolicies.control: Review of stock surplus eligibility.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria for inclusion in Tier 2 capital include issuance, subordination, maturity, and call features.
subpolicies.control: Compliance with Tier 2 criteria.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Investors must have no rights to accelerate repayment except in bankruptcy and liquidation.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring of investor rights.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Provisions held against future losses qualify for inclusion within Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Assessment of loan-loss reserves.

policies.policy_title: Limits on General Provisions for Tier 2 Capital
policies.policy_description: Establishes limits on the inclusion of general provisions and loan-loss reserves in Tier 2 capital.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks calculating credit risk-weighted assets under the standardized approach.
policies.objective: To ensure that general provisions do not exceed regulatory limits in Tier 2 capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Limits general provisions eligible for inclusion in Tier 2 to a maximum of 1.25 percentage points of credit risk-weighted risk assets.
subpolicies.control: Regulatory compliance with Basel II standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Recognition of Excess Provisions under IRB Approach

subpolicies.control: National discretion may apply to set a limit lower than 0.6%.

policies.policy_title: Recognition of Minority Interest in Common Equity Tier 1
