policies.scope: Applicable to banks with fully consolidated subsidiaries.
policies.objective: To ensure that minority interests meet the necessary criteria for inclusion in Common Equity Tier 1.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Criteria for Recognition of Minority Interest
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minority interest from common shares issued by a fully consolidated subsidiary may be recognized in Common Equity Tier 1 if the instrument meets all relevant criteria.
subpolicies.control: Compliance with regulatory requirements for capital instruments.

subpolicies.control: Monitoring credit growth trends and adjusting requirements as necessary.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Systemically important banks must have loss-absorbing capacity beyond minimum standards.
subpolicies.control: Ongoing assessment and development of methodologies for systemic importance.

policies.policy_description: Introduces globally harmonized liquidity standards to ensure banking sector stability.
policies.scope: Banking Sector
policies.objective: To promote a strong liquidity base and prevent liquidity crises.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Promotes resilience to liquidity disruptions over a thirty-day horizon.
subpolicies.control: Banks must hold sufficient high-quality liquid assets to cover net cash outflows.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires banks to maintain stable sources of funding relative to liquidity profiles of assets.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring of funding sources and liquidity profiles to ensure stability.

policies.policy_description: Aims to limit over-reliance on short-term wholesale funding and encourage better assessment of liquidity risk across all on- and off-balance sheet items.
policies.scope: Banking organizations and financial sector supervision
policies.objective: To ensure stable funding and mitigate liquidity risks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: A set of quantitative measures used to monitor liquidity risk profiles.
subpolicies.control: Use of common metrics for consistency in supervision.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Assessment of a bank's liquidity needs through contractual maturity mismatch.
subpolicies.control: Frequent assessments to compare liquidity risk profiles.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Analysis of concentrations of wholesale funding by specific counterparties and instruments.
subpolicies.control: Assessment of funding liquidity risks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Measurement of unencumbered assets available for secured funding.
subpolicies.control: Awareness of capacity to raise additional secured funds.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Assessment of the Liquidity Coverage Ratio in significant currencies.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring and managing currency exposure.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Monitoring market-wide data and institution-specific information for liquidity difficulties.
subpolicies.control: Use of instantaneous data sources.

policies.policy_description: Implementation of new standards to ensure the banking sector meets higher capital standards.
policies.scope: Banking sector during transition to Basel III standards
policies.objective: Support lending while meeting capital standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Introduction of the Liquidity Coverage Ratio starting January 1, 2015.
subpolicies.control: Rigorous reporting processes during the transition.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minimum standard for the Net Stable Funding Ratio by January 1, 2018.
subpolicies.control: Review of implications for financial markets and economic growth.

policies.policy_description: Establishes a clear definition of capital with a focus on common equity.
policies.scope: Global banking system
policies.objective: Ensure high-quality capital levels and consistency across jurisdictions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Total regulatory capital consists of Tier 1 and Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Criteria for inclusion in capital categories.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minimum capital ratios for Common Equity Tier 1, Tier 1 Capital, and Total Capital.
subpolicies.control: Restrictions on capital elements and regulatory adjustments.

policies.policy_description: Criteria for instruments to be included in Common Equity Tier 1 capital.
policies.scope: Applicable to internationally active banks structured as joint stock companies.
policies.objective: To ensure that Common Equity Tier 1 capital meets specific regulatory criteria.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Criteria for Classification as Common Shares
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the criteria that must be met for an instrument to be classified as common shares for regulatory capital purposes.
subpolicies.control: Criteria include subordinated claims, perpetual principal, non-obligatory distributions, and loss absorption.

policies.policy_description: Criteria for instruments to be included in Additional Tier 1 capital.
policies.scope: Applicable to instruments issued by the bank and its consolidated subsidiaries.
policies.objective: To establish minimum criteria for inclusion in Additional Tier 1 capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Criteria for Inclusion in Additional Tier 1 Capital
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Sets forth the minimum set of criteria for an instrument to be included in Additional Tier 1 capital.
subpolicies.control: Criteria include being issued and paid-in, subordinated, perpetual, callable under specific conditions, and having dividend/coupon discretion.

policies.policy_description: Regulations regarding the payment of dividends and coupons to common stockholders.
policies.scope: Applicable to banking organizations
policies.objective: Ensure that dividends and distributions are paid from distributable items and do not compromise the bank's financial stability.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Dividends/coupons must be paid out of distributable items.
subpolicies.control: Compliance with national insolvency law.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments cannot have a credit sensitive dividend feature.
subpolicies.control: Periodic resets based on the banking organization’s credit standing are prohibited.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments must not contribute to liabilities exceeding assets.
subpolicies.control: Compliance with national insolvency law balance sheet tests.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Replacement issues can occur concurrently with but not after the instrument is called.
subpolicies.control: Regulatory compliance.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minimum refers to the regulator’s prescribed minimum requirement.
subpolicies.control: Must meet or exceed Basel III Pillar 1 minimum requirement.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Full discretion at all times to cancel distributions/payments is required.
subpolicies.control: Prohibition of instruments with dividend pushers.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments classified as liabilities must have principal loss absorption.
subpolicies.control: Mechanisms include conversion to common shares or a write-down mechanism.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Neither the bank nor related parties can purchase the instrument.
subpolicies.control: No direct or indirect funding of the purchase by the bank.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments must not have features that hinder recapitalisation.
subpolicies.control: Provisions that require compensation to investors for lower-priced new instruments are prohibited.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Proceeds from instruments not issued out of an operating entity must be available without limitation.
subpolicies.control: Must meet criteria for inclusion in Additional Tier 1 capital.

policies.policy_description: Criteria and regulations for Tier 2 capital instruments.
policies.scope: Applicable to banking organizations
policies.objective: Provide loss absorption on a gone-concern basis.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minimum criteria for an instrument to be included in Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Instruments must be issued, subordinated, and meet maturity requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments may be callable only after a minimum of five years.
subpolicies.control: Requires prior supervisory approval and must not create expectations of call exercise.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Investors must have no rights to accelerate repayment except in bankruptcy.
subpolicies.control: Compliance with regulatory standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments cannot have a credit sensitive dividend feature.
subpolicies.control: Prohibition of dividends/coupons that reset based on credit standing.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Neither the bank nor related parties can purchase the instrument.
subpolicies.control: No direct or indirect funding of the purchase by the bank.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Proceeds from instruments not issued out of an operating entity must be available without limitation.
subpolicies.control: Must meet criteria for inclusion in Tier 2 capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Stock surplus not eligible for Tier 1 can be included in Tier 2 if criteria are met.
subpolicies.control: Shares giving rise to stock surplus must be permitted in Tier 2 capital.

policies.policy_description: Limits on general provisions and loan-loss reserves eligible for inclusion in Tier 2 capital.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks calculating credit risk-weighted risk assets under the standardized approach.
policies.objective: To ensure that general provisions do not exceed regulatory limits.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: General provisions eligible for Tier 2 capital are limited to a maximum of 1.25 percentage points of credit risk-weighted risk assets.
subpolicies.control: Compliance with the limit on general provisions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks may recognize the difference between total expected loss and total eligible provisions in Tier 2 capital up to a maximum of 0.6% of credit risk-weighted assets under the IRB approach.
subpolicies.control: National discretion may apply to set a limit lower than 0.6%.

policies.policy_description: Criteria for recognizing minority interest and capital issued by consolidated subsidiaries.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks with consolidated subsidiaries.
policies.objective: To establish guidelines for the recognition of minority interests in capital calculations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Recognition of Minority Interest from Common Shares
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minority interest from common shares issued by fully consolidated subsidiaries may receive recognition in capital.
subpolicies.control: Ensure proper accounting for minority interests in capital calculations.

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: Framework for the capitalisation of bank exposures to central counterparties and management of counterparty credit risk.
policies.scope: Banks and financial institutions
policies.objective: To establish strong standards for financial market infrastructures and mitigate systemic risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Capitalisation of Bank Exposures to CCPs
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Capitalisation based on compliance of CCPs with IOSCO standards, with low risk weights for compliant exposures.
subpolicies.control: Risk weight proposed at 2% for compliant CCPs.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Raising standards for counterparty credit risk management, including treatment of wrong-way risk.
subpolicies.control: Final guidance for sound backtesting of counterparty credit exposures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Internal Assessments of Externally Rated Securitisation Exposures
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for banks to perform internal assessments and mitigate reliance on external ratings.
subpolicies.control: Elimination of cliff effects and incorporation of IOSCO Code of Conduct elements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Introduction of a leverage ratio to constrain leverage in the banking sector and mitigate destabilising processes.
subpolicies.control: Calculated comparably across jurisdictions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Introduction of measures to dampen excess cyclicality of minimum capital requirements and promote forward-looking provisions.
subpolicies.control: Conservation of capital to build buffers for stress periods.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Safeguards to address excess cyclicality in minimum capital requirements, including long-term data horizons and stress testing.
subpolicies.control: Comprehensive data collection initiative to assess Basel II impact.

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: Framework to enhance the resilience of banks through improved capital requirements and provisioning practices.
policies.scope: Banking sector
policies.objective: To promote stronger capital conservation and provisioning practices to mitigate procyclicality and systemic risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Promoting stronger provisioning practices through an expected loss approach and updating supervisory guidance.
subpolicies.control: Implementation of an expected loss approach to improve financial reporting and transparency.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Introducing a framework to promote conservation of capital and build adequate buffers above minimum requirements.
subpolicies.control: Internationally agreed capital conservation standards to enhance sector resilience.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Adjusting capital buffer ranges in response to excessive credit growth to protect the banking sector.
subpolicies.control: Countercyclical buffer mechanism to address macroprudential goals.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Developing methodologies to assess systemic importance and enhance loss absorbency for systemically important banks.
subpolicies.control: Combination of capital surcharges, contingent capital, and bail-in debt for systemic institutions.

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: Framework for Tier 2 capital under Basel III regulations to enhance banking sector resilience.
policies.scope: Banks and financial institutions
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain adequate capital and liquidity to withstand financial stress.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Promotes resilience to potential liquidity disruptions over a thirty-day horizon by ensuring sufficient high-quality liquid assets.
subpolicies.control: Banks must hold unencumbered, high-quality liquid assets to offset net cash outflows during acute stress scenarios.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requires a minimum amount of stable sources of funding relative to the liquidity profiles of assets over a one-year horizon.
subpolicies.control: Encourages banks to limit reliance on short-term wholesale funding and assess liquidity risks comprehensively.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Establishes common metrics for supervisors to monitor liquidity risk profiles across banking organizations.
subpolicies.control: Supervisors should use a set of common metrics as minimum types of information for liquidity risk assessment.

section_info.title: consolidated subsidiaries that is held by third parties

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: A global regulatory framework for more resilient banks and banking systems.
policies.scope: Banks and banking systems globally
policies.objective: To enhance the resilience of banks and banking systems through improved capital standards and liquidity risk management.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks should frequently conduct a contractual maturity mismatch assessment to understand liquidity needs.
subpolicies.control: Frequency of assessments to compare liquidity risk profiles.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Analysis of concentrations of wholesale funding provided by specific counterparties, instruments, and currencies.
subpolicies.control: Assessment of funding liquidity risks in the event of funding source withdrawal.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Measurement of unencumbered assets available for collateral in secured funding.
subpolicies.control: Awareness of potential capacity to raise additional secured funds.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Assessment of LCR in each significant currency to monitor currency exposure.
subpolicies.control: Management of overall currency exposure at a bank.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Monitoring of market-wide data on asset prices, liquidity, and institution-specific funding information.
subpolicies.control: Use of instantaneous data to identify potential liquidity difficulties.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Introduction of transitional arrangements to implement new standards for capital requirements.
subpolicies.control: Rigorous reporting processes to monitor ratios during the transition period.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Observation Period for LCR and NSFR
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Both LCR and NSFR will undergo an observation period with a review clause for unintended consequences.
subpolicies.control: Continuous review of implications for financial markets and economic growth.

policies.policy_description: Application of minimum capital requirements following the Basel II Framework.
policies.scope: Banks and banking systems globally
policies.objective: To ensure consistency in the application of capital requirements.

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: Regulatory adjustments subject to specific capital requirements for banks.
policies.scope: Applies to banks, banking groups, and other entities whose capital is being measured.
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain adequate capital levels at all times.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Common Equity Tier 1 must be at least 4.5% of risk-weighted assets.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring and enforcement of capital ratios.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Tier 1 Capital must be at least 6.0% of risk-weighted assets.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring and enforcement of capital ratios.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Total Capital must be at least 8.0% of risk-weighted assets.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring and enforcement of capital ratios.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Criteria for Common Equity Tier 1 Capital
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the elements that constitute Common Equity Tier 1 capital.
subpolicies.control: Compliance with classification criteria for capital instruments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the elements that constitute Additional Tier 1 capital.
subpolicies.control: Compliance with classification criteria for capital instruments.

policies.policy_description: Criteria for an instrument issued by the bank to be included in Additional Tier 1 capital.
policies.scope: Instruments issued by the bank
policies.objective: To ensure that only qualifying instruments are included in Additional Tier 1 capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments must be issued and paid-in.
subpolicies.control: Must meet issuance criteria.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments must be subordinated to depositors and creditors.
subpolicies.control: Subordination must be legally enforceable.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments cannot be secured or guaranteed in a way that enhances seniority.
subpolicies.control: No legal or economic enhancements allowed.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments must be perpetual with no maturity date.
subpolicies.control: No step-ups or incentives to redeem allowed.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments may be callable only after five years with supervisory approval.
subpolicies.control: Must not create expectations for call exercise.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Any repayment must have prior supervisory approval.
subpolicies.control: No market expectations for approval.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Full discretion to cancel distributions at all times.
subpolicies.control: Cancellation must not be an event of default.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Dividends/coupons must be paid out of distributable items.
subpolicies.control: Payments must not create restrictions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments cannot have credit sensitive dividend features.
subpolicies.control: No reset based on credit standing.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments must not contribute to liabilities exceeding assets.
subpolicies.control: Must comply with national insolvency law.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments must have principal loss absorption features.
subpolicies.control: Must include conversion or write-down mechanisms.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Neither the bank nor related parties can purchase the instrument.
subpolicies.control: No funding for purchases allowed.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments must not hinder recapitalisation.
subpolicies.control: No compensation provisions for lower price issues.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Proceeds must be immediately available to operating entities.
subpolicies.control: Must meet all other criteria for inclusion.

policies.policy_description: Criteria for inclusion of instruments in Tier 2 capital.
policies.scope: Instruments issued by the bank and subsidiaries.
policies.objective: To define the elements that constitute Tier 2 capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments must meet criteria for inclusion in Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Must not be included in Tier 1 capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Stock surplus from Tier 2 instruments is included.
subpolicies.control: Must not be included in Common Equity Tier 1.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments issued by consolidated subsidiaries must meet criteria.
subpolicies.control: Must not be included in Tier 1 capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Certain loan loss provisions are included.
subpolicies.control: As specified in regulatory paragraphs.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Regulatory adjustments applied in the calculation of Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Must comply with regulatory standards.

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: Criteria for instruments to be included in Tier 2 capital as per Basel III.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and their issued instruments.
policies.objective: To ensure that instruments included in Tier 2 capital provide adequate loss absorption.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Minimum set of criteria for an instrument to meet or exceed for inclusion in Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Instruments must be issued and paid-in, subordinated, and meet maturity and callable conditions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Conditions regarding investor rights related to repayment and acceleration.
subpolicies.control: Investors must not have rights to accelerate repayment except in bankruptcy.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Restrictions on dividend features of the instrument.
subpolicies.control: The instrument cannot have a credit sensitive dividend feature.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Conditions under which the bank or related parties can purchase the instrument.
subpolicies.control: Neither the bank nor related parties can purchase or fund the purchase of the instrument.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for proceeds from instruments issued out of non-operating entities.
subpolicies.control: Proceeds must be immediately available to an operating entity or holding company.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Conditions under which general provisions can be included in Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Limited to a maximum of 1.25 percentage points of credit risk-weighted risk assets.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Excess Provisions under Internal Ratings-based Approach
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Recognition of excess provisions in Tier 2 capital under the IRB approach.
subpolicies.control: Banks may recognize the difference in Tier 2 capital up to a maximum of 0.6% of credit risk-weighted assets.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Conditions for recognizing minority interest from common shares issued by consolidated subsidiaries.
subpolicies.control: Must meet all criteria for Common Equity Tier 1 recognition.

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: Framework for transitional arrangements under Basel III for banks and banking systems.
policies.scope: Banks and banking systems
policies.objective: To establish guidelines for credit protection, ratings, and capital conservation.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Recognition of credit protection provided to securitisation exposures and the treatment under the Foundation IRB approach.
subpolicies.control: Requirements outlined in paragraphs 189 to 194 must be met.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must use chosen ECAIs and their ratings consistently and may use unsolicited ratings under certain conditions.
subpolicies.control: National supervisory authorities may allow the use of unsolicited ratings if they meet quality standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Operation of the capital conservation buffer to ensure banks build up capital outside periods of stress.
subpolicies.control: Banks should hold buffers above the regulatory minimum and reduce discretionary distributions to rebuild buffers.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Guidelines for banks to maintain capital buffers and strategies for rebuilding them.
subpolicies.control: Consider reducing dividends, share-backs, and bonuses, or raising new capital from the private sector.

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: Framework for assessing risk coverage under Basel III regulations.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and their consolidated subsidiaries.
policies.objective: To ensure that banks maintain adequate capital levels to cover risks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria for recognizing minority interest in consolidated Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Total minority interest minus surplus Common Equity Tier 1 attributable to minority shareholders.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria for recognizing Tier 1 capital instruments issued by consolidated subsidiaries.
subpolicies.control: Total Tier 1 of the subsidiary issued to third parties minus surplus Tier 1 attributable to third party investors.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria for recognizing total capital instruments issued by consolidated subsidiaries.
subpolicies.control: Total capital instruments of the subsidiary issued to third parties minus surplus Total Capital attributable to third party investors.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Treatment of capital issued to third parties through SPVs.
subpolicies.control: Capital can be included in Additional Tier 1 or Tier 2 if it meets entry criteria.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Adjustments to be applied to regulatory capital calculations.
subpolicies.control: Includes deductions for goodwill, intangibles, and deferred tax assets.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Deduction of goodwill and other intangibles from Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Full amount deducted net of associated deferred tax liability.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Deduction of deferred tax assets that rely on future profitability.
subpolicies.control: DTAs can be netted with associated DTLs if permitted by taxation authority.

policies.policy_description: Framework for managing risks related to operating losses, tax assets, and capital calculations.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and financial institutions.
policies.objective: To ensure accurate capital calculations and risk management concerning tax assets, provisions, and capital instruments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Deduction of Unused Tax Losses and Credits
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Unused tax losses and credits are to be deducted in full net of deferred tax liabilities.
subpolicies.control: Ensure DTLs netted against DTAs exclude amounts related to goodwill, intangibles, and defined benefit pension assets.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Claims or receivables from the government due to overinstallment of tax or current year tax losses carried back should be classified as current tax assets.
subpolicies.control: Assign relevant sovereign risk weighting to such claims.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The amount of the cash flow hedge reserve related to non-fair valued items should be derecognised in Common Equity Tier 1 calculations.
subpolicies.control: Positive amounts deducted and negative amounts added back.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Deduction from capital for shortfall of provisions to expected losses under the IRB approach.
subpolicies.control: Full amount deducted without tax effect reductions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Derecognise any increase in equity capital from securitisation transactions in Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Exclude expected future margin income gains.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: All unrealised gains and losses from changes in the fair value of liabilities due to the bank’s own credit risk must be derecognised.
subpolicies.control: Ensure accurate representation in Common Equity Tier 1.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defined benefit pension fund liabilities must be fully recognised in Common Equity Tier 1 calculations.
subpolicies.control: Deduct pension fund assets net of associated deferred tax liabilities.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: All investments in own common shares must be deducted in Common Equity Tier 1 calculations.
subpolicies.control: Apply deductions irrespective of exposure location and ensure no double counting.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Reciprocal cross holdings designed to inflate capital positions must be deducted in full.
subpolicies.control: Apply a corresponding deduction approach.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Investments in Capital of Other Entities
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Adjustments for investments in capital of banking, financial, and insurance entities outside regulatory consolidation.
subpolicies.control: Include direct, indirect, and synthetic holdings in capital calculations.

policies.policy_description: Regulatory adjustments for capital holdings and risk coverage in banks.
policies.scope: Banks and financial institutions
policies.objective: To ensure adequate capital coverage and manage risks associated with investments in other financial entities.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Deduction of holdings exceeding 10% of common equity from the respective capital tier.
subpolicies.control: Shortfalls in capital will be deducted from the next higher tier.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria for including investments in capital calculations, including direct, indirect, and synthetic holdings.
subpolicies.control: Holdings in both banking and trading books must be included.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Limited recognition for certain items when calculating Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Recognition is capped at 10% of common equity after regulatory adjustments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: National discretion allows temporary exclusion of certain investments with supervisory approval.
subpolicies.control: Applicable for investments made in the context of reorganizing distressed institutions.

policies.policy_description: Framework for managing risk coverage in relation to Common Equity Tier 1 capital and regulatory adjustments.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks under Basel III regulations.
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain adequate capital levels and transparency in regulatory capital disclosures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Items included in the 15% aggregate limit must not exceed 15% of Common Equity Tier 1 capital after regulatory adjustments.
subpolicies.control: Full disclosure of items included in the limit.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Non-deducted items in the calculation of Common Equity Tier 1 will be risk weighted at 250%.
subpolicies.control: Regulatory compliance for risk weighting.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must disclose a full reconciliation of regulatory capital elements and all regulatory adjustments.
subpolicies.control: Transparency in financial reporting.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Phased implementation of new capital standards from 1 January 2013 to 1 January 2018.
subpolicies.control: Compliance with phased capital requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Phasing Out of Non-Qualifying Capital Instruments
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Capital instruments that no longer qualify will be phased out beginning 1 January 2013, with a cap on recognition.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring and adjustment of capital instruments.

policies.policy_description: Framework for managing risk coverage related to capital instruments and counterparty credit risk.
policies.scope: Applicable to capital instruments and counterparty credit risk management.
policies.objective: To ensure all material risks are captured in the capital framework and to outline reforms to the counterparty credit risk framework.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments not meeting new criteria for inclusion in Tier 1 or Tier 2 will be derecognised from regulatory capital.
subpolicies.control: Instruments will be phased out from 1 January 2013 if they do not meet the criteria.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Reforms to the counterparty credit risk framework effective from 1 January 2013.
subpolicies.control: Implementation of revised metrics to address counterparty credit risk and related exposures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Capital charge to cover the risk of mark-to-market losses on expected counterparty risk (CVA) to OTC derivatives.
subpolicies.control: CVA capital charge will be calculated based on the bank’s approved method for counterparty credit risk.

policies.policy_title: Market Risk Internal Models Approach for CVA Calculation
policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the requirements for calculating the capital charge for counterparty credit risk using the market risk internal models approach.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks using internal models for market risk related to OTC derivatives.
policies.objective: To ensure accurate calculation of the CVA capital charge based on changes in counterparties' credit spreads.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Details the formula and components required for calculating the CVA of each counterparty, including expected exposure and loss given default.
subpolicies.control: Must adhere to the formula provided and use appropriate credit spreads for calculations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Specifies the requirement to use CDS spreads when available, or an appropriate proxy spread based on counterparty characteristics.
subpolicies.control: Banks must ensure the use of the most relevant credit spread data.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines how expected exposure should be calculated for different netting sets and the use of a constant EE profile for certain methods.
subpolicies.control: Banks must follow the guidelines for calculating expected exposure as per regulatory standards.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: CVA Risk Charge for Non-IMM Netting Sets
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Outlines how to include non-IMM netting sets into the CVA risk capital charge calculation.
subpolicies.control: Must assume a constant EE profile based on specified criteria.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: CVA Risk Charge for Specific Counterparty Exposures
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Describes the conditions under which the advanced CVA risk charge can be applied and the requirements for supervisory approval.
subpolicies.control: Only exposures with supervisory approval for specific risk modeling are eligible for the advanced CVA risk charge.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Details the requirements for calculating both non-stressed and stressed VaR components for CVA risk capital charge.
subpolicies.control: Must use current parameter calibrations for non-stressed VaR and stressed exposure parameter calibrations for stressed VaR.

policies.policy_description: Regulations regarding the calculation and management of the CVA risk capital charge for banks.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks calculating CVA risk capital charges for OTC derivatives counterparties.
policies.objective: To ensure that banks adequately cover CVA risks in their capital calculations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the types of hedges that can be included in the CVA risk capital charge calculation.
subpolicies.control: Only single-name CDSs, single-name contingent CDSs, and index CDSs are eligible, with specific restrictions on index CDSs.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Outlines the formula and requirements for banks without approvals to calculate a portfolio capital charge.
subpolicies.control: Banks must use a specified formula to calculate the CVA capital charge based on counterparty ratings and exposures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Aggregate CCR and CVA Risk Capital Charges
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Details the aggregation process for default risk capital charge and CVA risk capital charge.
subpolicies.control: The total CCR capital charge is determined by the higher of the IMM capital charge based on current or stressed parameters.

policies.policy_description: Framework for assessing and managing risks related to counterparty credit risk and wrong-way risk.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and financial institutions under Basel III regulations.
policies.objective: To ensure adequate capital charges and risk management practices for counterparty credit risk and specific wrong-way risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: CVA Risk Capital Charge for Banks with IMM Approval
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Determination of total CCR capital charge based on current and stressed parameter calibrations for EAD.
subpolicies.control: Higher of IMM capital charge based on current parameter calibrations or stressed parameter calibrations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: CVA Risk Capital Charge for All Other Banks
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Calculation of total CCR capital charge for banks not under IMM approval.
subpolicies.control: Sum of CEM or SM based capital charge and standardized CVA risk capital charge.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must identify exposures that give rise to general wrong-way risk and monitor them.
subpolicies.control: Stress testing and scenario analyses to identify risk factors correlated with counterparty creditworthiness.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Policies for identifying and managing specific wrong-way risk for each legal entity.
subpolicies.control: Separate rating and treatment of transactions with identified specific wrong-way risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: EAD Calculation for Specific Wrong-Way Risk
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Adjustment of EAD calculation for counterparties with identified specific wrong-way risk.
subpolicies.control: Use of full expected loss in remaining fair value for EAD calculation.

policies.policy_description: Framework for calculating risk-weighted assets and capital requirements for financial institutions.
policies.scope: Large financial institutions and collateralized counterparties
policies.objective: To implement risk coverage measures consistent with Basel framework revisions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: AVC Multiplier for Large Financial Institutions
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Revision of paragraph 272 of the Basel framework to include a multiplier of 1.25 for correlation parameter for regulated financial institutions with total assets >= US $100 billion.
subpolicies.control: Use of the most recent audited financial statement to determine asset size.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Implementation of increased margin periods of risk for netting sets based on transaction types and conditions.
subpolicies.control: Supervisory floors for margin periods based on transaction characteristics, including daily re-margining and trade volume.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Establishment of minimum holding periods for various transaction types based on daily re-margining.
subpolicies.control: Minimum holding periods adjusted according to specific transaction criteria outlined in the policy.

policies.policy_description: Policy outlining the minimum holding period for repo-style transactions and netting sets based on specified criteria.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks engaging in repo-style or similar transactions.
policies.objective: To establish a minimum holding period that reflects the liquidity of the concerned instruments and aligns with Basel II and III guidelines.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines the minimum holding period as the margin period of risk applicable under specified paragraphs of Annex 4.
subpolicies.control: Compliance with paragraphs 41(i), 41(ii), and 41(iii) of Annex 4.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Revision of the shortcut method for estimating Effective EPE for margined counterparties.
subpolicies.control: Adherence to the revised paragraph 41 of Annex 4 in Basel II and consideration of Basel III revisions.

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: Framework for managing counterparty credit risk in accordance with Basel III regulations.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and their consolidated subsidiaries.
policies.objective: To ensure that capital instruments issued by subsidiaries are recognized appropriately in regulatory capital calculations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria for recognizing minority interest in consolidated Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Calculation based on total minority interest minus surplus Common Equity Tier 1 attributable to minority shareholders.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Recognition of Tier 1 Capital from Subsidiaries
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Conditions under which Tier 1 capital instruments issued by subsidiaries are recognized.
subpolicies.control: Total Tier 1 of the subsidiary minus surplus Tier 1 attributable to third party investors.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Recognition of Total Capital from Subsidiaries
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Guidelines for recognizing total capital instruments issued by subsidiaries.
subpolicies.control: Total capital instruments of the subsidiary minus surplus Total Capital attributable to third party investors.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Treatment of capital issued to third parties through special purpose vehicles.
subpolicies.control: Capital can be included in Additional Tier 1 or Tier 2 if it meets relevant entry criteria.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Adjustments applied to regulatory capital calculations.
subpolicies.control: Includes deductions for goodwill, intangibles, and deferred tax assets.

policies.policy_description: Framework for managing counterparty credit risk in relation to capital calculations and deductions.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and financial institutions
policies.objective: To ensure accurate capital calculations and mitigate risks associated with counterparty credit.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Deferred tax liabilities must exclude amounts netted against goodwill, intangibles, and pension assets.
subpolicies.control: Pro rata allocation of deferred tax liabilities.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Derecognition of cash flow hedge reserves related to non-fair valued items in Common Equity Tier 1 calculations.
subpolicies.control: Adjustments for positive and negative amounts.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Shortfall of Provisions to Expected Losses
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Full deduction from capital for shortfalls in provisions under the IRB approach.
subpolicies.control: No tax effects considered in the deduction.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Derecognition of increases in equity capital from securitisation transactions in Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Exclusion of expected future margin income gains.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Unrealised Gains and Losses on Fair Valued Financial Liabilities
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Derecognition of unrealised gains and losses due to changes in the bank's own credit risk.
subpolicies.control: Impact on Common Equity Tier 1 calculations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Defined Benefit Pension Fund Assets and Liabilities
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Full recognition of pension fund liabilities in Common Equity Tier 1; deductions for assets net of deferred tax liabilities.
subpolicies.control: Offsetting assets allowed with supervisory approval.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: All investments in own common shares must be deducted in Common Equity Tier 1 calculations.
subpolicies.control: Consideration of direct and indirect holdings and contractual obligations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Full deduction of reciprocal cross holdings designed to inflate capital positions.
subpolicies.control: Corresponding deduction approach applied.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Investments in Capital of Banking Entities
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Regulatory adjustments for investments in capital of banking, financial, and insurance entities outside regulatory consolidation.
subpolicies.control: Inclusion of direct, indirect, and synthetic holdings.

policies.policy_description: Regulatory adjustments related to capital holdings and deductions for banks regarding counterparty credit risk.
policies.scope: Applies to banks holding Common Equity Tier 1, Additional Tier 1, or Tier 2 capital.
policies.objective: To ensure adequate capital deductions for investments exceeding specified thresholds.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Deductions from Common Equity Tier 1 capital for holdings exceeding 10% of the bank’s common equity.
subpolicies.control: Deductions applied based on the corresponding deduction approach.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Deductions from Additional Tier 1 capital for holdings exceeding 10% of the bank’s common equity.
subpolicies.control: Deductions applied based on the corresponding deduction approach.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Deductions from Tier 2 capital for holdings exceeding 10% of the bank’s common equity.
subpolicies.control: Deductions applied based on the corresponding deduction approach.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Limited recognition for significant investments in common shares capped at 10% of common equity.
subpolicies.control: Recognition of investments in common shares subject to specified thresholds.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Allows banks to use conservative estimates for monitoring exposures if operationally burdensome.
subpolicies.control: Requires prior supervisory approval.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Allows temporary exclusion of certain investments made for financial assistance to distressed institutions.
subpolicies.control: Requires prior supervisory approval.

policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the requirements and standards for managing counterparty credit risk in accordance with Basel III regulations.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banks and financial institutions under Basel III framework.
policies.objective: To ensure that banks maintain adequate capital against counterparty credit risk and improve transparency in regulatory capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The amount of three items recognized after regulatory adjustments must not exceed 15% of Common Equity Tier 1 capital.
subpolicies.control: Full disclosure of items included in the 15% aggregate limit.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Items not deducted from Common Equity Tier 1 will be risk weighted at 250%.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring and reporting of risk-weighted assets.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must disclose regulatory capital elements and adjustments clearly in financial statements.
subpolicies.control: Full reconciliation of regulatory capital to balance sheet.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Phased implementation of new capital requirements and regulatory adjustments from 2013 to 2018.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring compliance with phased-in capital requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Capital instruments that no longer qualify will be phased out starting from 2013, with gradual reductions in recognition.
subpolicies.control: Tracking of capital instruments and their compliance status.

policies.policy_description: Framework outlining the reforms to the counterparty credit risk management and capital framework.
policies.scope: Applicable to capital instruments and counterparty credit risk management.
policies.objective: To ensure that all material risks are captured in the capital framework and to raise the quality and level of the capital base.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Outlines the conditions under which capital instruments will be derecognised from Tier 1 or Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Instruments not called at their effective maturity date and not meeting new criteria will be derecognised.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Phasing Out of Additional Tier 1 and Tier 2 Instruments
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Details the phasing out process for instruments that no longer qualify as Additional Tier 1 or Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Instruments will be phased out from 1 January 2013 if they do not meet the new criteria.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Specifies conditions under which capital instruments will be excluded from Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Instruments must meet specific conditions to qualify for inclusion in Common Equity Tier 1.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Reforms to ensure that all material on- and off-balance sheet risks, including derivative related exposures, are captured.
subpolicies.control: Effective on 1 January 2013 to address failures in capturing major risks.

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: Regulatory adjustments to be applied to regulatory capital under Basel III.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks under Basel III framework
policies.objective: To ensure the resilience of banks by properly calculating regulatory capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Total capital instruments issued by fully consolidated subsidiaries may receive recognition in Total Capital if they meet classification criteria.
subpolicies.control: Calculation of Total Capital recognition based on surplus Total Capital attributable to third party investors.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Capital Issued Through Special Purpose Vehicles (SPVs)
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Capital issued to third parties through SPVs can be included in Additional Tier 1 or Tier 2 if it meets relevant criteria.
subpolicies.control: Assessment of SPV assets and compliance with entry criteria.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Goodwill and other intangibles must be deducted in the calculation of Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Full deduction of goodwill and intangibles, net of deferred tax liabilities.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: DTAs that rely on future profitability must be deducted in Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Netting of DTAs with DTLs only if permitted by taxation authority.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Cash flow hedge reserve related to non-fair valued items should be derecognised in Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Adjustment of positive and negative amounts in the hedge reserve.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Shortfall of Provisions to Expected Losses
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Full deduction from capital for shortfall of provisions to expected losses under the IRB approach.
subpolicies.control: No reduction by tax effects expected from provisions rising to expected losses.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Gain on Sale from Securitisation Transactions
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Increase in equity capital from securitisation transactions must be derecognised in Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Assessment of gains associated with expected future margin income.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Changes in Own Credit Risk on Fair Valued Financial Liabilities
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Unrealised gains and losses due to changes in the bank's own credit risk must be derecognised in Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring of fair value changes related to credit risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Defined Benefit Pension Fund Assets and Liabilities
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defined benefit pension fund liabilities must be fully recognised in Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Deduction of pension fund assets net of associated deferred tax liabilities.

policies.policy_description: Regulatory adjustments related to the treatment of assets and investments in a bank's capital calculations.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and financial institutions
policies.objective: To ensure proper accounting treatment and risk management of assets and investments to protect depositors and creditors.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Assets in the fund with unrestricted access can offset deductions with supervisory approval.
subpolicies.control: Assets must be shown to be easily withdrawn from the fund.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: All investments in a bank's own common shares must be deducted from Common Equity Tier 1.
subpolicies.control: Deduction applies irrespective of exposure location in banking or trading book.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Reciprocal cross holdings designed to inflate capital positions must be deducted in full.
subpolicies.control: Corresponding deduction approach to be applied.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Investments in Capital of Other Entities
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Investments in capital of banking, financial, and insurance entities outside regulatory consolidation must be deducted if exceeding 10%.
subpolicies.control: Include direct, indirect, and synthetic holdings; apply national discretion with supervisory approval.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Shortfall Deduction from Higher Tier Capital
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: If a bank lacks sufficient capital to satisfy a deduction, the shortfall will be deducted from the next higher tier of capital.
subpolicies.control: Ensure compliance with tiered capital structure.

policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the application of risk weighting for investments in the capital of banking, financial, and insurance entities that are outside the scope of regulatory consolidation.
policies.scope: Applies to banks with significant investments in entities outside regulatory consolidation.
policies.objective: To ensure proper risk weighting and deduction of investments in capital instruments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Investments include direct, indirect, and synthetic holdings of capital instruments, including holdings in both banking and trading books.
subpolicies.control: Banks must look through holdings of index securities to determine underlying capital holdings.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Investments not qualifying as common shares must be fully deducted from the same tier of capital for which they would qualify if issued by the bank.
subpolicies.control: Shortfalls in capital deductions must be deducted from the next higher tier of capital.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Certain items may receive limited recognition when calculating Common Equity Tier 1, capped at 10% of the bank’s common equity.
subpolicies.control: Items include significant investments in common shares of unconsolidated financial institutions, mortgage servicing rights, and DTAs from temporary differences.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must disclose a full reconciliation of regulatory capital elements and specific components benefiting from transitional provisions.
subpolicies.control: Disclosure must include separate adjustments and items not deducted from Common Equity Tier 1.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: National implementation of new standards to begin on 1 January 2013, with minimum capital requirements established.
subpolicies.control: Banks must meet new minimum requirements for Common Equity Tier 1, Tier 1 capital, and total capital relative to risk-weighted assets.

policies.policy_description: Establishes minimum capital requirements for banks to enhance resilience.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banks within the jurisdiction.
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain adequate capital levels to absorb losses.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Phased increase in minimum Common Equity Tier 1 requirements from 2% to 4.5% by 2015.
subpolicies.control: Compliance with phased capital requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Phased increase in Tier 1 capital requirements from 4% to 6% by 2015.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring of Tier 1 capital levels.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Regulatory Adjustments to Common Equity Tier 1
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Implementation of regulatory adjustments to Common Equity Tier 1, reaching 100% deduction by 2018.
subpolicies.control: Tracking of regulatory adjustments and compliance.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Phased Treatment of Capital from Subsidiaries
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Phased exclusion of capital issued out of subsidiaries held by third parties, reaching 100% exclusion by 2018.
subpolicies.control: Assessment of capital eligibility and adjustments.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Grandfathering of Public Sector Capital Injections
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Existing public sector capital injections will be grandfathered until 2018.
subpolicies.control: Monitoring of capital injection compliance.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Gradual phasing out of capital instruments that no longer qualify as non-common equity Tier 1 or Tier 2 capital.
subpolicies.control: Management of capital instrument recognition and amortization.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Exclusion of Certain Instruments from Common Equity Tier 1
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Instruments not meeting criteria for inclusion in Common Equity Tier 1 will be excluded starting January 2013.
subpolicies.control: Verification of instrument eligibility.

policies.policy_description: Reforms to ensure all material risks are captured in the capital framework.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banks managing counterparty credit risk.
policies.objective: To address major on- and off-balance sheet risks and derivative exposures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Implementation of reforms to capture counterparty credit risk effectively.
subpolicies.control: Regular assessment of risk management practices.

section_info.title: Asset value correlation multiplier for large financial institutions

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_title: Asset Value Correlation Multiplier for Large Financial Institutions
policies.policy_description: A framework to determine the default risk capital charge for counterparty credit risk for IMM banks using Effective EPE with current and stressed market data.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks with permission to use the internal models method (IMM) for calculating counterparty credit risk (CCR) regulatory capital.
policies.objective: To enhance the measurement and management of counterparty credit risk through improved calibration of Effective EPE.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Banks must use the greater of the portfolio-level capital charge based on Effective EPE using current market data and the stress calibration.
subpolicies.control: Effective EPE must be calculated on a total portfolio level, not on a counterparty basis.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The Effective EPE model must be calibrated using current market data and at least three years of historical data, or market implied data.
subpolicies.control: Data must be updated quarterly or more frequently if market conditions warrant.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: The bank must demonstrate the adequacy of the stress calibration by assessing credit spreads and mapping counterparties to credit spread data.
subpolicies.control: The exposure model must include data from the stressed credit period and be consistent with the Effective EPE model calibration.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: To evaluate stress calibration effectiveness, the bank must create benchmark portfolios vulnerable to main risk factors.
subpolicies.control: Exposures of benchmark portfolios must be calculated using current positions and stressed inputs from the 3-year stress period.

section_info.title: Collateralised counterparties and margin period of risk

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_title: Collateralised Counterparties and Margin Period of Risk
policies.policy_description: Regulatory framework for the treatment of counterparty credit risk and capital charges related to credit value adjustments (CVA) on OTC derivatives.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks under Basel III framework
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain adequate capital to cover potential CVA losses on counterparty credit risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for calculating the capital charge for mark-to-market losses on expected counterparty risk.
subpolicies.control: Banks must model the impact of changes in counterparties’ credit spreads on CVA using approved methods.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Advanced CVA Risk Capital Charge for Banks with IMM Approval
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Calculation requirements for banks with Internal Models Method (IMM) approval using specific interest rate risk VaR models.
subpolicies.control: Banks must use a specific formula to calculate CVA based on credit spreads and expected exposures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Details on the formula and parameters to be used in calculating CVA for counterparties.
subpolicies.control: Banks must adhere to the prescribed formula for CVA calculations and ensure accuracy in inputs.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Short Cut Method for Margined Trades
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Guidelines for banks using the short cut method for collateralised OTC derivatives.
subpolicies.control: Banks must compute CVA risk capital charge assuming a constant expected exposure profile.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for including non-IMM netting sets into the CVA risk capital charge.
subpolicies.control: Banks must assume a constant expected exposure profile for non-IMM netting sets in their calculations.

policies.policy_title: Collateralised Counterparties and Margin Period of Risk
policies.policy_description: Guidelines for calculating CVA risk capital charges for collateralised and uncollateralised OTC derivatives counterparties.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banks calculating CVA risk capital charges.
policies.objective: To ensure appropriate reflection of credit spread risks in the bank's market risk VaR model.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Use of Advanced CVA Risk Charge
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Conditions under which the advanced CVA risk charge can be applied, specifically regarding supervisory approval for modelling specific risks.
subpolicies.control: Only exposures with supervisory approval for modelling specific risk are included.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Calculation of CVA Risk Capital Charge
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Methodology for calculating the CVA risk capital charge, including components of general and specific credit spread risks.
subpolicies.control: CVA risk capital charge must be calculated as a standalone market risk charge.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Criteria for hedges that can be included in the CVA risk capital charge calculation.
subpolicies.control: Only specific types of CDS and equivalent hedging instruments are eligible.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Calculation methodology for banks without approvals to use advanced CVA risk charge.
subpolicies.control: Portfolio capital charge must be calculated using specified formulas and weights based on counterparty ratings.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Mapping of counterparties to weights based on external ratings for the calculation of CVA risk capital charges.
subpolicies.control: Counterparties without external ratings must be mapped to internal ratings with supervisory approval.

policies.policy_title: Collateralised Counterparties and Margin Period of Risk
policies.policy_description: Framework for determining CVA risk capital charge for potential mark-to-market losses and default risk capital charges.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks with IMM approval and market-risk internal-models approval.
policies.objective: To establish a methodology for calculating the total CCR capital charge for banks based on different capital charge approaches.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Determination of the IMM capital charge based on current and stressed parameter calibrations for EAD.
subpolicies.control: Higher of the IMM capital charge based on current or stressed parameters.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Calculation of risk weights applied to OTC derivative exposures for IRB banks with full maturity adjustment.
subpolicies.control: Risk weights calculated with PD and M set equal to 1, contingent on demonstration to national supervisor.

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_description: Framework for determining the total CCR capital charge for banks based on their approval status and risk assessments.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banks operating with central counterparties under Basel III regulations.
policies.objective: To ensure banks maintain adequate capital buffers against counterparty credit risk.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Capital Charge for Banks with IMM Approval
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Determination of total CCR capital charge for banks with IMM approval and without Specific Risk VaR approval for bonds.
subpolicies.control: Sum of the higher of IMM capital charge based on current or stressed parameter calibrations for EAD and the standardized CVA risk capital charge.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Capital Charge for All Other Banks
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Determination of total CCR capital charge for banks not covered by specific approvals.
subpolicies.control: Sum over all counterparties of the CEM or SM based capital charge with EADs determined by specified paragraphs and the standardized CVA risk capital charge.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition and calculation of outstanding EAD for OTC derivative counterparties.
subpolicies.control: Defined as the greater of zero and the difference between the sum of EADs across all netting sets and the recognized CVA loss.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for banks to identify exposures that give rise to general wrong-way risk.
subpolicies.control: Stress testing and scenario analyses to monitor wrong-way risk by product, region, and industry.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Policies for identifying and managing specific wrong-way risk for counterparties.
subpolicies.control: Procedures to identify, monitor, and control specific wrong-way risk from inception to trade lifecycle.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Implementation of the AVC multiplier for large financial institutions.
subpolicies.control: Revisions to the formula for calculating risk-weighted assets based on PD and LGD.

policies.policy_description: Defines the capital requirement (K) for defaulted exposures and the calculation of risk-weighted assets (RWA).
policies.scope: Applicable to banks and financial institutions managing defaulted exposures.
policies.objective: To ensure adequate capital is maintained against defaulted exposures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: K is calculated as the greater of zero and the difference between LGD and expected loss.
subpolicies.control: Ensure accurate estimation of LGD and expected loss.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: A multiplier of 1.25 is applied to the correlation parameter for exposures to regulated financial institutions with total assets ≥ $100 billion.
subpolicies.control: Use the most recent audited financial statements to determine asset size.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Negative Capital Charge for Sovereign Exposure
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: If the calculation results in a negative capital charge for sovereign exposure, apply a zero capital charge.
subpolicies.control: Monitor sovereign exposures to ensure compliance.

policies.policy_title: Margin Period of Risk for Collateralised Counterparties
policies.policy_description: Implements increased margin periods of risk for transactions subject to daily re-margining.
policies.scope: Applicable to banks engaging in repo-style transactions and other netting sets.
policies.objective: To mitigate risks associated with collateralised transactions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Supervisory Floor for Margin Period of Risk
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Imposes a supervisory floor of five business days for repo-style transactions and ten business days for other netting sets.
subpolicies.control: Assess the number of trades and the nature of collateral to determine appropriate supervisory floors.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Increased Margin Period for High Trade Volume
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Imposes a supervisory floor of 20 business days for netting sets with more than 5,000 trades in a quarter.
subpolicies.control: Track trade volumes and adjust margin periods accordingly.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: If more than two margin call disputes occur over two quarters, the margin period must be at least double the supervisory floor.
subpolicies.control: Document and review margin call disputes to ensure compliance.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Replaces minimum holding periods for various transaction types based on margin period of risk.
subpolicies.control: Ensure that holding periods align with the defined margin periods of risk.

policies.policy_description: This policy outlines the method for modeling Expected Positive Exposure (EPE) for counterparties that do not have margin agreements.
policies.scope: Applicable to institutions that model EPE without margin agreements.
policies.objective: To provide a framework for modeling EPE for margined counterparties.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Describes the specific method to be used for modeling EPE in the context of margined counterparties.
subpolicies.control: Ensure compliance with Basel III revisions published in December 2017.

section_info.title: Enhanced counterparty credit risk management requirements

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities. This framework provides structured guidance for policy development, implementation, and monitoring across various business functions.

policies.policy_title: Enhanced Counterparty Credit Risk Management Requirements
policies.policy_description: Requirements for managing counterparty credit risk in accordance with Basel III standards.
policies.scope: Applicable to all banks managing counterparty credit risk.
policies.objective: To enhance the resilience of banks by implementing robust counterparty credit risk management practices.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Total CCR Capital Charge for Banks with IMM Approval
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Determination of total CCR capital charge based on IMM capital charge and standardised CVA risk capital charge.
subpolicies.control: Higher of IMM capital charge based on current or stressed parameter calibrations.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Total CCR Capital Charge for All Other Banks
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Calculation of total CCR capital charge as the sum of CEM or SM based capital charge and standardised CVA risk capital charge.
subpolicies.control: EADs determined by specified paragraphs.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Definition of outstanding EAD for OTC derivative counterparties and calculation of RWAs.
subpolicies.control: Outstanding EAD calculated as applicable risk weight multiplied by the outstanding EAD.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Requirements for identifying exposures that give rise to general wrong-way risk and conducting stress testing.
subpolicies.control: Regular reports to senior management on wrong-way risks.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Procedures for identifying, monitoring, and controlling specific wrong-way risk for each legal entity.
subpolicies.control: EAD calculations adjusted for specific wrong-way risk exposures.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Revisions to the calculation of risk-weighted assets to implement the asset value correlation multiplier.
subpolicies.control: Correlation calculated using specified formula.

policies.policy_title: Enhanced Counterparty Credit Risk Management Requirements
policies.policy_description: Requirements for managing counterparty credit risk, including capital requirements and margin periods.
policies.scope: Financial institutions and banks
policies.objective: To enhance the management of counterparty credit risk and ensure adequate capital requirements.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Maturity Adjustment and Capital Requirement Calculation
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Details the formula for calculating maturity adjustment and capital requirements based on loss given default (LGD) and probability of default (PD).
subpolicies.control: Calculation of capital requirement (K) and risk-weighted assets (RWA) based on specified formulas.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Specifies a multiplier for the correlation parameter for exposures to regulated and unregulated financial institutions.
subpolicies.control: Application of a multiplier of 1.25 for regulated financial institutions with total assets ≥ US $100 billion.

subpolicies.subpolicy_title: Margin Period of Risk for Collateralised Counterparties
subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Establishes minimum margin periods of risk for different types of transactions and conditions.
subpolicies.control: Implementation of supervisory floors for margin periods based on transaction types and conditions.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Increases margin period of risk for netting sets with a history of margin call disputes.
subpolicies.control: Use of a margin period of risk that is at least double the supervisory floor for netting sets with more than two disputes.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Defines minimum holding periods for various transaction types based on re-margining frequency.
subpolicies.control: Minimum holding periods adjusted based on transaction type and margin period of risk.

policies.policy_description: Requirements for managing counterparty credit risk with a focus on the shortcut method for margined counterparties.
policies.scope: Applies to banks that model Exposure at Default (EPE) without margin agreements.
policies.objective: To provide a method for banks to manage credit risk for margined counterparties when higher modeling sophistication is not achievable.

subpolicies.subpolicy_description: Allows banks to use a simplified method for calculating EPE for margined counterparties.
subpolicies.control: Compliance with Basel III revisions published in December 2017.

framework_info.framework_description: A comprehensive policy framework designed to establish governance, compliance, and operational standards for organizational activities.
